<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b><u>Lenz's Law</u></b>.</div><div>- The current-carrying wire induces its own magnetic field in response the changing number of magentic flux lines passing through its surface.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""f4a11fd7a41001b13ade6230ff2512d0.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2d5c79a3dd881007955d048fcdd3e668.png""></div></div>"
"As the <b>magnetic flux (φ<sub>B</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span> through a current-carrying loop, the <i><u>induced</u> </i><b>magnetic flux</b> points in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;opposite&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[same/opposite]</span> direction."	"As the <b>magnetic flux (φ<sub>B</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span> through a current-carrying loop, the <i><u>induced</u> </i><b>magnetic flux</b> points in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>opposite</i></span> direction.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b><u>Lenz's Law</u></b>.</div><div>- The current-carrying wire induces its own magnetic field in response the changing number of magentic flux lines passing through its surface.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""f4a11fd7a41001b13ade6230ff2512d0.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2d5c79a3dd881007955d048fcdd3e668.png""></div></div>"
"As the <b>magnetic flux (φ<sub>B</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreases]</span> through a current-carrying loop, the <i><u>induced</u> </i><b>magnetic flux</b> points in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>same</i></span> direction."	"As the <b>magnetic flux (φ<sub>B</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreases</i></span> through a current-carrying loop, the <i><u>induced</u> </i><b>magnetic flux</b> points in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>same</i></span> direction.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b><u>Lenz's Law</u></b>.</div><div>- The current-carrying wire induces its own magnetic field in response the changing number of magentic flux lines passing through its surface.</div><div>- Direction of the current induces a flux that results in a ""net decrease"" in flux.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""f4a11fd7a41001b13ade6230ff2512d0.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2d5c79a3dd881007955d048fcdd3e668.png""></div></div>"
"As the <b>magnetic flux (φ<sub>B</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreases</i></span> through a current-carrying loop, the <i><u>induced</u> </i><b>magnetic flux</b> points in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[same/opposite]</span> direction."	"As the <b>magnetic flux (φ<sub>B</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreases</i></span> through a current-carrying loop, the <i><u>induced</u> </i><b>magnetic flux</b> points in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>same</i></span> direction.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b><u>Lenz's Law</u></b>.</div><div>- The current-carrying wire induces its own magnetic field in response the changing number of magentic flux lines passing through its surface.</div><div>- Direction of the current induces a flux that results in a ""net decrease"" in flux.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""f4a11fd7a41001b13ade6230ff2512d0.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2d5c79a3dd881007955d048fcdd3e668.png""></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>magnetic forces</b></span> exerted on <i>each other</i> by <i>two</i> parallel <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">current</span>-carrying wires:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;21b501b0d0cacb36ae16627f9b8e7ed3&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>magnetic forces</b></span> exerted on <i>each other</i> by <i>two</i> parallel <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">current</span>-carrying wires:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""21b501b0d0cacb36ae16627f9b8e7ed3.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>When currents have the same direction → attractive</div><div>When currents have opposite directions → repulsive</div><div><br></div><img src=""ed7603114c90a667509138d046feecbd.png""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;magnetic&#x20;forces&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> exerted on <i>each other</i> by <i>two</i> parallel <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""current"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>-carrying wires:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""21b501b0d0cacb36ae16627f9b8e7ed3.png""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>magnetic forces</b></span> exerted on <i>each other</i> by <i>two</i> parallel <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">current</span>-carrying wires:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""21b501b0d0cacb36ae16627f9b8e7ed3.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>When currents have the same direction → attractive</div><div>When currents have opposite directions → repulsive</div><div><br></div><img src=""ed7603114c90a667509138d046feecbd.png""></div>"
"<div>When two parallel <u>current</u>-carrying wires point in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;the&amp;nbsp&#x3B;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[the same/opposite]</span> direction(s), the <b>magnetic force</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">attractive</span>.</div>"	"<div>When two parallel <u>current</u>-carrying wires point in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>the&nbsp;same</i></span> direction(s), the <b>magnetic force</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">attractive</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The magnetic force is <u>generated</u> by these two currents.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""21b501b0d0cacb36ae16627f9b8e7ed3.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""ed7603114c90a667509138d046feecbd.png""></div>"
"<div>When two parallel <u>current</u>-carrying wires point in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>the&nbsp;same</i></span> direction(s), the <b>magnetic force</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""attractive"" data-ordinal=""2"">[attractive/repulsive]</span>.</div>"	"<div>When two parallel <u>current</u>-carrying wires point in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>the&nbsp;same</i></span> direction(s), the <b>magnetic force</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">attractive</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The magnetic force is <u>generated</u> by these two currents.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""21b501b0d0cacb36ae16627f9b8e7ed3.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""ed7603114c90a667509138d046feecbd.png""></div>"
"<div>When two parallel <u>current</u>-carrying wires point in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;opposite&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[the same/opposite]</span> direction(s), the <b>magnetic force</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">repulsive</span>.</div>"	"<div>When two parallel <u>current</u>-carrying wires point in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>opposite</i></span> direction(s), the <b>magnetic force</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">repulsive</span>.</div>

<div>The magnetic force is <u>generated</u> by these two currents.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""21b501b0d0cacb36ae16627f9b8e7ed3.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""ed7603114c90a667509138d046feecbd.png""></div>"
"<div>When two parallel <u>current</u>-carrying wires point in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>opposite</i></span> direction(s), the <b>magnetic force</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""repulsive"" data-ordinal=""2"">[attractive/repulsive]</span>.</div>"	"<div>When two parallel <u>current</u>-carrying wires point in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>opposite</i></span> direction(s), the <b>magnetic force</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">repulsive</span>.</div>

<div>The magnetic force is <u>generated</u> by these two currents.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""21b501b0d0cacb36ae16627f9b8e7ed3.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""ed7603114c90a667509138d046feecbd.png""></div>"
"A <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;dielectric&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is an insulating material between two conducting plates in a <u>capacitor</u>."	"A <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric</b></span> is an insulating material between two conducting plates in a <u>capacitor</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Dielectric <u>constant</u></b> of the material is represented with ""<b>κ</b>.""</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""cfd784030804de2f5cb8f05f6a9de34c.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Dielectric&#x20;breakdown&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[process]</span> occurs in capacitors when a strong <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">electric field</span> <i>ionizes</i> the atoms in the dielectric material - turning it into a <u>conductor</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Dielectric breakdown</b></span> occurs in capacitors when a strong <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">electric field</span> <i>ionizes</i> the atoms in the dielectric material - turning it into a <u>conductor</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Positive charges in the dielectric → attracted to negatively charged plate.</div><div>Negative charges in the dielectric → attracted to positively charged plate.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Lichtenberg_figure_in_block_of_Plexiglas.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""cfd784030804de2f5cb8f05f6a9de34c.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Dielectric breakdown</b></span> occurs in capacitors when a strong <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""electric&#x20;field"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>ionizes</i> the atoms in the dielectric material - turning it into a <u>conductor</u>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Dielectric breakdown</b></span> occurs in capacitors when a strong <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">electric field</span> <i>ionizes</i> the atoms in the dielectric material - turning it into a <u>conductor</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Positive charges in the dielectric → attracted to negatively charged plate.</div><div>Negative charges in the dielectric → attracted to positively charged plate.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Lichtenberg_figure_in_block_of_Plexiglas.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""cfd784030804de2f5cb8f05f6a9de34c.png""></div>"
"<div>Due to <b>dielectric breakdown</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;positively&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span> charged particles in the ionized dielectric material are <i>attracted </i>to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>anode</u></span> (<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">-</span>).</div>"	"<div>Due to <b>dielectric breakdown</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positively</i></span> charged particles in the ionized dielectric material are <i>attracted </i>to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>anode</u></span> (<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">-</span>).</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Batteries are galvanic cells (anode is negative; cathode is positive)</div><div><br></div><div>Positive charges in the dielectric → attracted to negatively charged plate.</div><div>Negative charges in the dielectric → attracted to positively charged plate.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Lichtenberg_figure_in_block_of_Plexiglas.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""cfd784030804de2f5cb8f05f6a9de34c.png""></div>"
"<div>Due to <b>dielectric breakdown</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positively</i></span> charged particles in the ionized dielectric material are <i>attracted </i>to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;anode&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[anode/cathode]</span> (<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2D;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/-]</span>).</div>"	"<div>Due to <b>dielectric breakdown</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positively</i></span> charged particles in the ionized dielectric material are <i>attracted </i>to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>anode</u></span> (<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">-</span>).</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Batteries are galvanic cells (anode is negative; cathode is positive)</div><div><br></div><div>Positive charges in the dielectric → attracted to negatively charged plate.</div><div>Negative charges in the dielectric → attracted to positively charged plate.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Lichtenberg_figure_in_block_of_Plexiglas.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""cfd784030804de2f5cb8f05f6a9de34c.png""></div>"
"<div>When a circuit is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;closed&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[open/closed]</span>, a <u>current</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is</i></span> able to flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6ef01e9263bb860f1a1eb561adc0cd21.png"">"	"<div>When a circuit is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closed</i></span>, a <u>current</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is</i></span> able to flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6ef01e9263bb860f1a1eb561adc0cd21.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Open switch → current cannot flow.</div><div>- Closed switch → current can flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""0d3784bf6be2b302637cbd2563ee8c88.png""></div>"
"<div>Ohm's Law:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;c566e824f108110ad67be7667d7802231b9e4b5e&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Ohm's Law:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-c566e824f108110ad67be7667d7802231b9e4b5e.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""c5fa09aa5a97459af153e588d7601f91.png""></div>"
"In circuits, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>current (I)</b></span>&nbsp;point(s) from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cathode"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span>&nbsp;→ <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""anode"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span>."	"In circuits, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>current (I)</b></span>&nbsp;point(s) from the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cathode</span>&nbsp;→ <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">anode</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Cathode is the positively charged end, and the current points in the same direction positive charges <u>would</u> flow (not that they literally flow however...)</div><div><br></div><img src=""0777da696b3e62b7028a2e3d9a83bc7d.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Internal&#x20;resistance&#x20;&#x28;r&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the resistance generated from a small resistor <i>within </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>emf source (battery)</u></span> itself."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Internal resistance (r)</b></span> is the resistance generated from a small resistor <i>within </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>emf source (battery)</u></span> itself.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The actual voltage supplied to a cell is slightly less than it's able to do theoretically.</div><div>- Add internal resistance (r) to the other resistor (R) in the circuit to find the <i>total </i>resistance (R<sub>tot</sub> = R + r).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-8c9f6508c260784ff6b4ff5944dd5892c01dba44.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8e89ad1e75534d4280ef50f8edb75f43.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Internal resistance (r)</b></span> is the resistance generated from a small resistor <i>within </i>the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;emf&#x20;source&#x20;&#x28;battery&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> itself."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Internal resistance (r)</b></span> is the resistance generated from a small resistor <i>within </i>the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>emf source (battery)</u></span> itself.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The actual voltage supplied to a cell is slightly less than it's able to do theoretically.</div><div>- Add internal resistance (r) to the other resistor (R) in the circuit to find the <i>total </i>resistance (R<sub>tot</sub> = R + r).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-8c9f6508c260784ff6b4ff5944dd5892c01dba44.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8e89ad1e75534d4280ef50f8edb75f43.png""><br></div></div>"
"To find the <i>total </i>resistance (R<sub>tot</sub>) in a circuit, take the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""sum"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of its <b><i>internal </i>resistance (r)</b> &amp; <b><i>external </i>resistance (R)</b>."	"To find the <i>total </i>resistance (R<sub>tot</sub>) in a circuit, take the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">sum</span> of its <b><i>internal </i>resistance (r)</b> &amp; <b><i>external </i>resistance (R)</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>R<sub>tot</sub> = r + R</div><div><br></div><div>Internal resistance (r): Resistance inside the emf source itself.</div><div>External resistance (R): Resistance from a resistor in the circuit.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8e89ad1e75534d4280ef50f8edb75f43.png""><br></div></div>"
"In an <b>RC circuit</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;closing&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[opening/closing]</span> the switch causes the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>current (I)</u></span> to instantaneously <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span>."	"In an <b>RC circuit</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closing</i></span> the switch causes the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>current (I)</u></span> to instantaneously <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Closing the switch ""connects"" the circuit, allowing electrons to begin flowing (hence why it jumps up after being closed).</div><div>- Slowly decreases over time because charges accumulate on each side of the plate &amp; they start to repel each other. Shown below in (c).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6db5d2cadd819cc40fb7936cfffeaa4f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""0d3784bf6be2b302637cbd2563ee8c88.png""></div>"
"In an <b>RC circuit</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closing</i></span> the switch causes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;current&#x20;&#x28;I&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to instantaneously <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increase/decrease]</span>."	"In an <b>RC circuit</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closing</i></span> the switch causes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>current (I)</u></span> to instantaneously <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Closing the switch ""connects"" the circuit, allowing electrons to begin flowing (hence why it jumps up after being closed).</div><div>- Slowly decreases over time because charges accumulate on each side of the plate &amp; they start to repel each other. Shown below in (c).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6db5d2cadd819cc40fb7936cfffeaa4f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""0d3784bf6be2b302637cbd2563ee8c88.png""></div>"
"In RC circuits, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>time constant (τ)</b></span> is the <i><u>product</u></i> of (<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""resistance"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>) x (<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""capacitance"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>)."	"In RC circuits, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>time constant (τ)</b></span> is the <i><u>product</u></i> of (<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">resistance</span>) x (<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">capacitance</span>).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Hence why the time constant (<b>τ</b>) is also represented as ""<b>RC</b>"" in the below equations.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1edd65d7354a70f4d96483f421d951b2.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6db5d2cadd819cc40fb7936cfffeaa4f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""0d3784bf6be2b302637cbd2563ee8c88.png""></div>"
"In RC circuits, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;time&#x20;constant&#x20;&#x28;τ&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is the <i><u>product</u></i> of (<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">resistance</span>) x (<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">capacitance</span>)."	"In RC circuits, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>time constant (τ)</b></span> is the <i><u>product</u></i> of (<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">resistance</span>) x (<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">capacitance</span>).

"<b>Capacitors</b> with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;larger&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[larger/smaller]</span> areas require <i>more </i>time to <u>charge</u> fully."	"<b>Capacitors</b> with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> areas require <i>more </i>time to <u>charge</u> fully.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...since they can hold more total charge (q).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1edd65d7354a70f4d96483f421d951b2.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6db5d2cadd819cc40fb7936cfffeaa4f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""0d3784bf6be2b302637cbd2563ee8c88.png""></div>"

</div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>specific gravity</b></span> of a material in water:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;095b9246bf2c636849c6fd163144d69c&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>specific gravity</b></span> of a material in water:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""095b9246bf2c636849c6fd163144d69c.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Compares the density of the material (ρ) to water's density (ρ<sub>water</sub>).</div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;specific&#x20;gravity&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of a material in water:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""095b9246bf2c636849c6fd163144d69c.png""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>specific gravity</b></span> of a material in water:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""095b9246bf2c636849c6fd163144d69c.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Compares the density of the material (ρ) to water's density (ρ<sub>water</sub>).</div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;specific&#x20;gravity&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a material is a <i>comparison </i>of the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>material's</u>&nbsp;density (ρ)</span> relative to&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>water's</u>&nbsp;density (ρ)</span> - indicating if it floats or sinks."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>specific gravity</b></span> of a material is a <i>comparison </i>of the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>material's</u>&nbsp;density (ρ)</span> relative to&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>water's</u>&nbsp;density (ρ)</span> - indicating if it floats or sinks.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Compares the density of the material (ρ) to water's density (ρ<sub>water</sub>).<div>- Value greater than 1 (material's ρ is larger than water) means it sinks.<br><div><br></div><div><img src=""095b9246bf2c636849c6fd163144d69c.png""><br></div></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>specific gravity</b></span> of a material is a <i>comparison </i>of the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;material&#x27;s&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;density&#x20;&#x28;ρ&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> relative to&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;water&#x27;s&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;density&#x20;&#x28;ρ&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - indicating if it floats or sinks."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>specific gravity</b></span> of a material is a <i>comparison </i>of the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>material's</u>&nbsp;density (ρ)</span> relative to&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>water's</u>&nbsp;density (ρ)</span> - indicating if it floats or sinks.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Compares the density of the material (ρ) to water's density (ρ<sub>water</sub>).<div>- Value greater than 1 (material's ρ is larger than water) means it sinks.<br><div><br></div><div><img src=""095b9246bf2c636849c6fd163144d69c.png""><br></div></div></div>"
"In the equation for <b>specific gravity</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>density </i>of water (ρ<sub>water</sub>)</span>&nbsp;is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1000"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""kg&#x2F;m&lt;sup&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[units]</span> at 4°C."	"In the equation for <b>specific gravity</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>density </i>of water (ρ<sub>water</sub>)</span>&nbsp;is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1000</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">kg/m<sup>3</sup></span> at 4°C.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Compares the density of the material (ρ) to water's density (ρ<sub>water</sub>).<div>- Value greater than 1 (material's ρ is larger than water) means it sinks.<br><div><br></div><div><img src=""095b9246bf2c636849c6fd163144d69c.png""><br></div></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pressure</b></span> <i>exerted </i>on an object.</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;905b7fe06d00ea885004d3801af8c330&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pressure</b></span> <i>exerted </i>on an object.</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""905b7fe06d00ea885004d3801af8c330.png""></span><br>

<b>Force </b>(F) per unit of <b>area </b>(A).</div>"

"<div><b>Atmospheric pressure</b> is measured in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""atmospheres&#x20;&#x28;atm&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[units]</span>, since pascals (Pa) are <i>too small</i> for practical measurement.<br></div>"	"<div><b>Atmospheric pressure</b> is measured in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">atmospheres (atm)</span>, since pascals (Pa) are <i>too small</i> for practical measurement.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1 atm = 1 x 10<sup>5</sup> Pascals.</div><div>- Pressure equation shown below...</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""905b7fe06d00ea885004d3801af8c330.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Air pressure</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> at <i>high </i>altitudes.</div>"	"<div><b>Air pressure</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span> at <i>high </i>altitudes.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Low altitudes (underwater) have high pressure due to bearing the ""weight"" of everything above it plus the atmospheric pressure.</div><div>- High altitudes (mountains) have low pressure due to not having to bear the ""weight"" of everything above it.<br></div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Pressure&nbsp;</b>equation shown below... (force [F], area [A]).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""905b7fe06d00ea885004d3801af8c330.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ac7229cfddaa2b81e4091cf84ca073a3.png""></div></div>"
"<b>Air pressure</b>&nbsp;is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> at&nbsp;<i>low&nbsp;</i>altitudes."	"<b>Air pressure</b>&nbsp;is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> at&nbsp;<i>low&nbsp;</i>altitudes.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Low altitudes (underwater) have high pressure due to bearing the ""weight"" of everything above it plus the atmospheric pressure.</div><div>- High altitudes (mountains) have low pressure due to not having to bear the ""weight"" of everything above it.<br></div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Pressure&nbsp;</b>equation shown below... (force [F], area [A]).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""905b7fe06d00ea885004d3801af8c330.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ac7229cfddaa2b81e4091cf84ca073a3.png""></div></div>"
"The applied&nbsp;<b>pressure</b> to a fluid will be distrubuted <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;evenly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[unevenly/evenly]</span> throughout the <i>entire </i>fluid."	"The applied&nbsp;<b>pressure</b> to a fluid will be distrubuted <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>evenly</i></span> throughout the <i>entire </i>fluid.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>Pascal's principle</u></b></div><div>- Shown in the hydraulic image below.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""17083349730834061d4704f4b705c5cd.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a4f6b9b233e524aca976976a151f085f.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>absolute (<i>total</i>) pressure</b></span> exerted on an object submerged in a fluid:<br></div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;17083349730834061d4704f4b705c5cd&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>absolute (<i>total</i>) pressure</b></span> exerted on an object submerged in a fluid:<br></div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""17083349730834061d4704f4b705c5cd.png""></span></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- P&nbsp;is the total (absolute) pressure something experiences submerged in a fluid.<br></div><div>- P<sub>0</sub>&nbsp;is the pressure exerted on the surface of the fluid (most of the time it's the same as atmospheric pressure (1 x 10<sup>5</sup> Pa).</div><div>- h&nbsp;is the ""range"" (h<sub>2</sub> - h<sub>1</sub>) of depth of the fluid layer.</div><div>- ρ is the density of the fluid.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""50ae871fd77d20fe79bac2ad944547bb.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a4f6b9b233e524aca976976a151f085f.png""></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;absolute&#x20;&#x28;&lt;i&gt;total&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x29;&#x20;pressure&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> exerted on an object submerged in a fluid:<br></div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""17083349730834061d4704f4b705c5cd.png""></span></div></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>absolute (<i>total</i>) pressure</b></span> exerted on an object submerged in a fluid:<br></div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""17083349730834061d4704f4b705c5cd.png""></span></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- P&nbsp;is the total (absolute) pressure something experiences submerged in a fluid.<br></div><div>- P<sub>0</sub>&nbsp;is the pressure exerted on the surface of the fluid (most of the time it's the same as atmospheric pressure (1 x 10<sup>5</sup> Pa).</div><div>- h&nbsp;is the ""range"" (h<sub>2</sub> - h<sub>1</sub>) of depth of the fluid layer.</div><div>- ρ is the density of the fluid.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""50ae871fd77d20fe79bac2ad944547bb.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a4f6b9b233e524aca976976a151f085f.png""></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;gauge&#x20;pressure&#x20;&#x28;P&lt;sub&gt;gauge&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""2f80ab39e757a1d30b6119d5acce7227.png""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>gauge pressure (P<sub>gauge</sub>)</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""2f80ab39e757a1d30b6119d5acce7227.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Gauge pressure</b> represents the difference between the absolute pressure (i.e. total pressure experienced by something in a fluid) versus the outside atmospheric pressure.<div>- P<sub>0</sub>&nbsp;is the pressure exerted on the surface of the fluid (most of the time it's the same as atmospheric pressure (1 x 10<sup>5</sup>&nbsp;Pa).<br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>gauge pressure (P<sub>gauge</sub>)</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;2f80ab39e757a1d30b6119d5acce7227&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>gauge pressure (P<sub>gauge</sub>)</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""2f80ab39e757a1d30b6119d5acce7227.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Gauge pressure</b> represents the difference between the absolute pressure (i.e. total pressure experienced by something in a fluid) versus the outside atmospheric pressure.<div>- P<sub>0</sub>&nbsp;is the pressure exerted on the surface of the fluid (most of the time it's the same as atmospheric pressure (1 x 10<sup>5</sup>&nbsp;Pa).<br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;photoelectric&#x20;effect&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> describes how <u>photons of light</u> with particular frequencies are capable of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>ejecting </i>electrons</span> off of metals."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>photoelectric effect</b></span> describes how <u>photons of light</u> with particular frequencies are capable of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>ejecting </i>electrons</span> off of metals.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Induces a <b>current (I)</b>, since a current is the measure of electron flow over time.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>photoelectric effect</b></span> describes how <u>photons of light</u> with particular frequencies are capable of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;ejecting&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;electrons"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> off of metals."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>photoelectric effect</b></span> describes how <u>photons of light</u> with particular frequencies are capable of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>ejecting </i>electrons</span> off of metals.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Induces a <b>current (I)</b>, since a current is the measure of electron flow over time.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"<div>From the photoelectric effect, <u>photons of light</u> with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> intensities generate <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>larger</i></span> <b>currents (I)</b> from the ejected electrons.<br></div>"	"<div>From the photoelectric effect, <u>photons of light</u> with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> intensities generate <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>larger</i></span> <b>currents (I)</b> from the ejected electrons.<br></div>

"<div>From the photoelectric effect, <u>photons of light</u> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> intensities generate <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;larger&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[smaller/larger]</span> <b>currents (I)</b> from the ejected electrons.<br></div>"	"<div>From the photoelectric effect, <u>photons of light</u> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> intensities generate <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>larger</i></span> <b>currents (I)</b> from the ejected electrons.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"<div>From the photoelectric effect, <u>photons of light</u> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> intensities generate <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;smaller&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[smaller/larger]</span> <b>currents (I)</b> from the ejected electrons.<br></div>"	"<div>From the photoelectric effect, <u>photons of light</u> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> intensities generate <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>smaller</i></span> <b>currents (I)</b> from the ejected electrons.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In the photoelectric effect, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;threshold&#x20;frequency&#x20;&#x28;f&lt;sub&gt;T&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the <u><i>minimum </i>frequency</u> needed for the photon to <i>eject </i>an electron.</div>"	"<div>In the photoelectric effect, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>threshold frequency (f<sub>T</sub>)</b></span> is the <u><i>minimum </i>frequency</u> needed for the photon to <i>eject </i>an electron.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Frequency is directly proportional to the energy of the photon (below). If the threshold frequency is not met, it means the photon <u>does not</u> have enough energy to eject the electron.</div><div>- Threshold frequencies (f<sub>T</sub>) depend on the type of metal the light is hitting.</div><div>- Threshold energy is the minumum energy for it to be ejected (calculated using f<sub>T</sub>)... E=hf<sub>T</sub></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7531b9cbf1e71721d78a8ddd0b1bf92f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;maximum&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;kinetic&#x20;energy&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> imparted to an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;ejected&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;electron"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> due to the <b>photoelectric effect</b>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""81a8840b533469b6e4100c337dbe8207.png""></span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>maximum&nbsp;</i>kinetic energy</u></span> imparted to an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>ejected </i>electron</span> due to the <b>photoelectric effect</b>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""81a8840b533469b6e4100c337dbe8207.png""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The ""left-over"" energy from the photon is imparted as kinetic energy to the electron.</div><div>- (E=hf) is the equation for the amount of energy carried by a photon of light... (E=hf<sub>T</sub>) represents the minimum amount of energy needed for the electron to get ejected (threshold).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7531b9cbf1e71721d78a8ddd0b1bf92f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>maximum&nbsp;</i>kinetic energy</u></span> imparted to an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>ejected </i>electron</span> due to the <b>photoelectric effect</b>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;81a8840b533469b6e4100c337dbe8207&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>maximum&nbsp;</i>kinetic energy</u></span> imparted to an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>ejected </i>electron</span> due to the <b>photoelectric effect</b>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""81a8840b533469b6e4100c337dbe8207.png""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The ""left-over"" energy from the photon is imparted as kinetic energy to the electron.</div><div>- (E=hf) is the equation for the amount of energy carried by a photon of light... (E=hf<sub>T</sub>) represents the minimum amount of energy needed for the electron to get ejected (threshold).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7531b9cbf1e71721d78a8ddd0b1bf92f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"Waves with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> <b>frequencies (f)</b> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> <u>energies (E)</u> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <u>wavelengths (λ)</u>."	"Waves with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <b>frequencies (f)</b> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> <u>energies (E)</u> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <u>wavelengths (λ)</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"Waves with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <b>frequencies (f)</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <u>energies (E)</u> and <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <u>wavelengths (λ)</u>." "<div>In the equation for the <u><i>maximum </i>kinetic energy</u> imparted to an ejected electron in the photoelectric&nbsp;effect, <b>hf </b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""photon&#x27;s&#x20;energy"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""81a8840b533469b6e4100c337dbe8207.png"">"	"<div>In the equation for the <u><i>maximum </i>kinetic energy</u> imparted to an ejected electron in the photoelectric&nbsp;effect, <b>hf </b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">photon's energy</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""81a8840b533469b6e4100c337dbe8207.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The ""left-over"" energy from the photon is imparted as kinetic energy to the electron.</div><div>- Work function (E=hf<sub>T</sub>) is the amount of energy needed to eject the electron from the metal (since it uses the threshold frequency (f<sub>T</sub>) in its equation).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7531b9cbf1e71721d78a8ddd0b1bf92f.png""><br></div><div><img src=""f3d8f1e6fa681ca832b62dc94a73a3fd.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In the equation for the <u><i>maximum </i>kinetic energy</u> imparted to an ejected electron in the photoelectric&nbsp;effect, <b>hf<sub>T</sub>&nbsp;</b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;minumum&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;energy&#x20;needed&#x20;for&#x20;the&#x20;electron&#x20;to&#x20;be&#x20;ejected"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""81a8840b533469b6e4100c337dbe8207.png"">"	"<div>In the equation for the <u><i>maximum </i>kinetic energy</u> imparted to an ejected electron in the photoelectric&nbsp;effect, <b>hf<sub>T</sub>&nbsp;</b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>minumum </i>energy needed for the electron to be ejected</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""81a8840b533469b6e4100c337dbe8207.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The ""left-over"" energy from the photon is imparted as kinetic energy to the electron.</div><div>- Work function (E=hf<sub>T</sub>) is the amount of energy needed to eject the electron from the metal (since it uses the threshold frequency (f<sub>T</sub>) in its equation).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7531b9cbf1e71721d78a8ddd0b1bf92f.png""><br></div><div><img src=""f3d8f1e6fa681ca832b62dc94a73a3fd.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Photoelectric-Effect.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;mass&#x20;defect&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> refers to the fact that the mass of an element's nucleus weighs <i>slightly less</i> than the sum of its <u>protons</u> and <u>neutrons</u>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>mass defect</b></span> refers to the fact that the mass of an element's nucleus weighs <i>slightly less</i> than the sum of its <u>protons</u> and <u>neutrons</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- In reality, the difference in energy is <u>converted to energy</u> (shown below), which is the energy used to hold the nucleus together (<b>binding energy</b>).</div><div><br></div><img src=""dbc75c411fc548e419b09b77bbdc2f51.png""></div>"
"<div>Equation for an atom's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>binding energy (E)</b></span> - representing the amount of energy needed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>hold together</i> atomic nuclei</u></span> that arises when mass is <i>converted</i> into energy:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;dbc75c411fc548e419b09b77bbdc2f51&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Equation for an atom's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>binding energy (E)</b></span> - representing the amount of energy needed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>hold together</i> atomic nuclei</u></span> that arises when mass is <i>converted</i> into energy:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""dbc75c411fc548e419b09b77bbdc2f51.png""></span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Binding energy</b> of the nucleus.</div><div>- A little bit of mass gets converted into this type of energy (explaining the<b> mass defect</b>).</div><div>- In reality, the difference in mass is <u>converted to energy</u> (shown below), which is the energy used to hold the nucleus together.</div></div>"
"<div>Equation for an atom's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;binding&#x20;energy&#x20;&#x28;E&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - representing the amount of energy needed to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;hold&#x20;together&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;atomic&#x20;nuclei&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> that arises when mass is <i>converted</i> into energy:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""dbc75c411fc548e419b09b77bbdc2f51.png""></span></div>"	"<div>Equation for an atom's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>binding energy (E)</b></span> - representing the amount of energy needed to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>hold together</i> atomic nuclei</u></span> that arises when mass is <i>converted</i> into energy:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""dbc75c411fc548e419b09b77bbdc2f51.png""></span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Binding energy</b> of the nucleus.</div><div>- A little bit of mass gets converted into this type of energy (explaining the<b> mass defect</b>).</div><div>- In reality, the difference in mass is <u>converted to energy</u> (shown below), which is the energy used to hold the nucleus together.</div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;strong&#x20;nuclear&#x20;force&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is a fundamental force that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>draws</i>&nbsp;protons &amp; neutrons</u></span>&nbsp;toward each other&nbsp;when they're brought into close proximity."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>strong nuclear force</b></span> is a fundamental force that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>draws</i>&nbsp;protons &amp; neutrons</u></span>&nbsp;toward each other&nbsp;when they're brought into close proximity.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

This force is capable of overcoming the repulsive electromagnetic forces.</div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>strong nuclear force</b></span> is a fundamental force that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;draws&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;protons&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;neutrons&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;toward each other&nbsp;when they're brought into close proximity."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>strong nuclear force</b></span> is a fundamental force that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>draws</i>&nbsp;protons &amp; neutrons</u></span>&nbsp;toward each other&nbsp;when they're brought into close proximity.

<div>...because alpha decay is the emission of a helium packet.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""399dabe77d985ff7484cb7da1d640eec.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""b5eb60fef9bed59b3b4a573b0ad3ddca.png""><br></div></div>"

<div>...because alpha decay is the emission of a helium packet.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""399dabe77d985ff7484cb7da1d640eec.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""b5eb60fef9bed59b3b4a573b0ad3ddca.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;beta&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>decay</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">+1</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>beta<sup>-</sup></b></span> <b>decay</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">+1</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Beta<sup>-</sup> decay</b> is when a neutron is converted into a proton (releasing an electron and anti-neutrino).</div><div><br></div><img src=""194b7a85d3159bcb870d217d18c4e5a5.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>beta<sup>-</sup></b></span> <b>decay</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2B;1"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/- #]</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unchanged"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/- #]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>beta<sup>-</sup></b></span> <b>decay</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">+1</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Beta<sup>-</sup> decay</b> is when a neutron is converted into a proton (releasing an electron and anti-neutrino).</div><div><br></div><img src=""194b7a85d3159bcb870d217d18c4e5a5.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;beta&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;size&#x3A;&#x20;16&#x2E;6667px&#x3B;&quot;&gt;&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>decay</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">-1</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>beta<span style=""font-size: 16.6667px;""><sup>+</sup></span></b></span> <b>decay</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">-1</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Beta<sup>+</sup> decay</b> is when a proton is converted into a neutron (releasing a positron &amp; neutrino).</div><div><br></div><img src=""194b7a85d3159bcb870d217d18c4e5a5.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>beta<span style=""font-size: 16.6667px;""><sup>+</sup></span></b></span> <b>decay</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2D;1"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/- #]</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unchanged"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/- #]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>beta<span style=""font-size: 16.6667px;""><sup>+</sup></span></b></span> <b>decay</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">-1</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Beta<sup>+</sup> decay</b> is when a proton is converted into a neutron (releasing a positron &amp; neutrino).</div><div><br></div><img src=""194b7a85d3159bcb870d217d18c4e5a5.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In <b>electron capture</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2D;1"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/- #]</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unchanged"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/- #]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <b>electron capture</b>, the <u>atomic numbers (Z)</u> of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">-1</span> &amp; the <u>mass numbers (A)</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Electron capture</b>&nbsp;is when an electron is captured by the nucleus to convert a proton into a neutron.</div><div><br></div><img src=""194b7a85d3159bcb870d217d18c4e5a5.jpg""></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;beta&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;<b>decay</b>, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>neutron</u></span> is <i>converted into</i> a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>proton</u></span> - <i>emitting </i>a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">electron</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">anti-neutrino</span>."	"In <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>beta<sup>-</sup></b></span>&nbsp;<b>decay</b>, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>neutron</u></span> is <i>converted into</i> a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>proton</u></span> - <i>emitting </i>a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">electron</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">anti-neutrino</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>γ-rays are emitted, which are high-energy packets that do not result in any lowerings of the parent nuclei.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b5c0120b76fe7d4192430f075d5061d2.png""></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>gamma</b></span>&nbsp;<b>decay</b>, the&nbsp;<u>atomic numbers (Z)</u>&nbsp;of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unchanged"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/- #]</span> &amp; the&nbsp;<u>mass numbers (A)</u>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unchanged"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/- #]</span>."	"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>gamma</b></span>&nbsp;<b>decay</b>, the&nbsp;<u>atomic numbers (Z)</u>&nbsp;of daughter nuclei are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span> &amp; the&nbsp;<u>mass numbers (A)</u>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">unchanged</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>γ-rays are emitted, which are high-energy packets that do not result in any lowerings of the parent nuclei.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b5c0120b76fe7d4192430f075d5061d2.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Gamma&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>decay </b>is when <i>excited</i> parent nuclei <u>relieve themselves of energy</u> by emitting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">high-energy γ-rays (photons)</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gamma</b></span> <b>decay </b>is when <i>excited</i> parent nuclei <u>relieve themselves of energy</u> by emitting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">high-energy γ-rays (photons)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>γ-rays are emitted, which are high-energy packets that do not result in any lowerings of the parent nuclei.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b5c0120b76fe7d4192430f075d5061d2.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gamma</b></span> <b>decay </b>is when <i>excited</i> parent nuclei <u>relieve themselves of energy</u> by emitting <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""high&#x2D;energy&#x20;γ&#x2D;rays&#x20;&#x28;photons&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gamma</b></span> <b>decay </b>is when <i>excited</i> parent nuclei <u>relieve themselves of energy</u> by emitting <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">high-energy γ-rays (photons)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>γ-rays are emitted, which are high-energy packets that do not result in any lowerings of the parent nuclei.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b5c0120b76fe7d4192430f075d5061d2.png""></div>"
"<div>In radioactive decay, the sample's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;half&#x2D;life&#x20;&#x28;t&lt;sub&gt;1&#x2F;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the <u>amount of time</u> it takes for <i>half </i>of the sample to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">decay into its <i>daughter</i> nuclei</span>.</div>"	"<div>In radioactive decay, the sample's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>half-life (t<sub>1/2</sub>)</b></span> is the <u>amount of time</u> it takes for <i>half </i>of the sample to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">decay into its <i>daughter</i> nuclei</span>.</div>

<div><b>n</b> = number of nuclei that have not yet decayed; <b>t</b> = lapse of time; <b>λ</b> = decay constant (0.7/T<sub>1/2</sub>).</div><div><br></div><img src=""e7debbc58abc464a4621a09b214a4a46.png""></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>n</b> = number of nuclei that have not yet decayed; <b>t</b> = lapse of time; <b>λ</b> = decay constant (0.7/T<sub>1/2</sub>).</div><div><br></div><img src=""e7debbc58abc464a4621a09b214a4a46.png""></div>"

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

"In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;standing&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[standing/travel.]</span> <b>waves</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>nodes</u></span> are the points along the string where the amplitude is <i>zero</i>."	"In <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>standing</b></span> <b>waves</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>nodes</u></span> are the points along the string where the amplitude is <i>zero</i>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>Nodes and antinodes are <u>only found in standing waves</u> - the analogous points on travelling waves are called crests and troughs.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""674f100155a53630ef29fab436439068.png""><br></div></div></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>standing</b></span> <b>waves</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;nodes&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> are the points along the string where the amplitude is <i>zero</i>."	"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>standing</b></span> <b>waves</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>nodes</u></span> are the points along the string where the amplitude is <i>zero</i>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>Nodes and antinodes are <u>only found in standing waves</u> - the analogous points on travelling waves are called crests and troughs.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""674f100155a53630ef29fab436439068.png""><br></div></div></div>"
"In&nbsp;<b>waves</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;antinodes&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> are the points along the string where the amplitude is at its <i>highest</i>."	"In&nbsp;<b>waves</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>antinodes</u></span> are the points along the string where the amplitude is at its <i>highest</i>.

"Our <b>ears </b>can hear <u>frequencies</u> between <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""20"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> Hz to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""20&#x2C;000"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> Hz." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div><br></div></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>speed </i>of sound</b></span> travelling <i>through </i>a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">medium (solid, liquid, gas)</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;90fb590216e39b3ddef373ec34676b8d&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>speed </i>of sound</b></span> travelling <i>through </i>a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">medium (solid, liquid, gas)</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""90fb590216e39b3ddef373ec34676b8d.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>- <b>B</b> = bulk modulus (medium's resistance to compression); <b>ρ</b> = density of the medium.</div><div>- Sounds <u>cannot</u> travel through vacuums.</div></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;speed&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;of&#x20;sound&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> travelling <i>through </i>a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""medium&#x20;&#x28;solid&#x2C;&#x20;liquid&#x2C;&#x20;gas&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""90fb590216e39b3ddef373ec34676b8d.png""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>speed </i>of sound</b></span> travelling <i>through </i>a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">medium (solid, liquid, gas)</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""90fb590216e39b3ddef373ec34676b8d.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>- <b>B</b> = bulk modulus (medium's resistance to compression); <b>ρ</b> = density of the medium.</div><div>- Sounds <u>cannot</u> travel through vacuums.</div></div></div>"
"<div>In the equation for the <u>speed of sound</u> travelling through a medium, <b>B</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;bulk&#x20;modulus&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, which is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;medium&#x27;s&#x20;&lt;i&gt;resistance&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;to&#x20;compression&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""90fb590216e39b3ddef373ec34676b8d.png"">"	"<div>In the equation for the <u>speed of sound</u> travelling through a medium, <b>B</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>bulk modulus</b></span>, which is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>medium's <i>resistance </i>to compression</u></span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""90fb590216e39b3ddef373ec34676b8d.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>- Solids have the largest B, while gases have the smallest.</div><div>- <b>B</b> = bulk modulus (medium's resistance to compression); <b>ρ</b> = density of the medium.</div><div>- Sounds <u>cannot</u> travel through vacuums.</div></div></div>"
"<div>In the equation for the <u>speed of sound</u> travelling through a medium, <b>ρ</b>&nbsp;is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""medium&#x27;s&#x20;density"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""90fb590216e39b3ddef373ec34676b8d.png"">"	"<div>In the equation for the <u>speed of sound</u> travelling through a medium, <b>ρ</b>&nbsp;is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">medium's density</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""90fb590216e39b3ddef373ec34676b8d.png"">

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Solids&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[gas/solid]</span> have the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>highest</i></span> <b>bulk modulus</b>&nbsp;<b>(B)</b>, while <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;gases&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[gas/solid]</span> have the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lowest</i></span> bulk modulus."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>Solids</u></span> have the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>highest</i></span> <b>bulk modulus</b>&nbsp;<b>(B)</b>, while <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>gases</u></span> have the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lowest</i></span> bulk modulus.

"Due to the <b>doppler effect</b>, a sound source moving <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;towards&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[away from/towards]</span> you has a perceived <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> <u>frequency</u> &amp; a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> <u>pitch</u>."	"Due to the <b>doppler effect</b>, a sound source moving <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>towards</i></span> you has a perceived <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> <u>frequency</u> &amp; a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> <u>pitch</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Sound wave is ""compressed"" (↓λ, ↑f)</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>f'</b> = observer's perceived frequency; <b>f</b> = real frequency; <b>v</b> = speed of sound (343 m/s in 20°C); <b>v<sub>D</sub></b> = detector's (observer's) speed; <b>v<sub>S</sub></b> = speed of moving source.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-972e257dfae45d0aa145163f5fb4deba7cd343c0.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-c82370dbcc23ea6731f5157a0e77d1e946201b66.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Due to the <b>doppler effect</b>, a sound source moving <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>towards</i></span> you has a perceived <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <u>frequency</u> &amp; a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <u>pitch</u>."	"Due to the <b>doppler effect</b>, a sound source moving <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>towards</i></span> you has a perceived <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> <u>frequency</u> &amp; a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> <u>pitch</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Sound wave is ""compressed"" (↓λ, ↑f)</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>f'</b> = observer's perceived frequency; <b>f</b> = real frequency; <b>v</b> = speed of sound (343 m/s in 20°C); <b>v<sub>D</sub></b> = detector's (observer's) speed; <b>v<sub>S</sub></b> = speed of moving source.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-972e257dfae45d0aa145163f5fb4deba7cd343c0.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-c82370dbcc23ea6731f5157a0e77d1e946201b66.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Due to the <b>doppler effect</b>, a sound source moving <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;away&#x20;from&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[away from/towards]</span> you has a perceived <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> <u>frequency</u> &amp; a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> <u>pitch</u>."	"Due to the <b>doppler effect</b>, a sound source moving <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>away from</i></span> you has a perceived <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> <u>frequency</u> &amp; a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> <u>pitch</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Sound wave is ""stretched"" (↑λ, ↓f)</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>f'</b> = observer's perceived frequency; <b>f</b> = real frequency; <b>v</b> = speed of sound (343 m/s in 20°C); <b>v<sub>D</sub></b> = detector's (observer's) speed; <b>v<sub>S</sub></b> = speed of moving source.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9ff0e294ea1974304899a4246c232d3f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-c82370dbcc23ea6731f5157a0e77d1e946201b66.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Due to the <b>doppler effect</b>, a sound source moving <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>away from</i></span> you has a perceived <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <u>frequency</u> &amp; a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <u>pitch</u>."	"Due to the <b>doppler effect</b>, a sound source moving <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>away from</i></span> you has a perceived <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> <u>frequency</u> &amp; a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> <u>pitch</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Sound wave is ""stretched"" (↑λ, ↓f)</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>f'</b> = observer's perceived frequency; <b>f</b> = real frequency; <b>v</b> = speed of sound (343 m/s in 20°C); <b>v<sub>D</sub></b> = detector's (observer's) speed; <b>v<sub>S</sub></b> = speed of moving source.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9ff0e294ea1974304899a4246c232d3f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-c82370dbcc23ea6731f5157a0e77d1e946201b66.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Shock&#x20;waves&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are formed when an object travels <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>faster</i></span> than the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>speed of sound</u></span> - causing <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>front-loading</i> of its sound waves' amplitudes</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Shock waves</b></span> are formed when an object travels <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>faster</i></span> than the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>speed of sound</u></span> - causing <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>front-loading</i> of its sound waves' amplitudes</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Notice how the front portion of the sound waves ""stack up"" - leading to higher amplitudes (constructive interference).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""clipboard_e4533666e1df42a5c0da289aefe14a615.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""3f4fb97f56b4a093789c0ff77450ac09.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""64650bbbbb479d68bc081c575a1fb04c.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Shock waves</b></span> are formed when an object travels <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;faster&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[slower/faster/equal]</span> than the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;speed&#x20;of&#x20;sound&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - causing <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>front-loading</i> of its sound waves' amplitudes</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Shock waves</b></span> are formed when an object travels <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>faster</i></span> than the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>speed of sound</u></span> - causing <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>front-loading</i> of its sound waves' amplitudes</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Notice how the front portion of the sound waves ""stack up"" - leading to higher amplitudes (constructive interference).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""clipboard_e4533666e1df42a5c0da289aefe14a615.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""3f4fb97f56b4a093789c0ff77450ac09.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""64650bbbbb479d68bc081c575a1fb04c.png""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Shock waves</b></span> are formed when an object travels <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>faster</i></span> than the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>speed of sound</u></span> - causing <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>front-loading</i> of its sound waves' amplitudes</span>.

"<div>To find the <u>angle (θ)</u> a <b>shock wave</b> makes from an object in the air with respect to someone on the ground, use <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""sin&#x28;θ&#x29;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;v&lt;sub&gt;sound&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x2F;&#x20;v&lt;sub&gt;object&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""4"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>To find the <u>angle (θ)</u> a <b>shock wave</b> makes from an object in the air with respect to someone on the ground, use <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">sin(θ) = v<sub>sound</sub> / v<sub>object</sub></span>.<br></div>

"In optics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>virtual</b></span> <b>images </b>are when it <i>appears </i>that light is converging at an <u>imaginary point </u><i><u>behind</u></i> a mirror (not really converging there).

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Because of the angle of the reflected light off the mirror (below), it appears as if the light converges on a point behind the mirror.<br></div><div>- <b>Real</b>: light converges in front of the image (always inverted).</div><div><br></div><img src=""61111c6efea1d791bab3f6ed8848d2c0.png""></div>"
"<div>Plane <u>mirrors</u> form <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;virtual&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[real/virtual]</span> images.<br></div>"	"<div>Plane <u>mirrors</u> form <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>virtual</i></span> images.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<div>Concave <u>mirrors</u> form <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;real&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[real/virtual]</span> images.<br></div>"	"<div>Concave <u>mirrors</u> form <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>real</i></span> images.<br></div>

"In optics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;real&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[real/virtual]</span>&nbsp;<b>images&nbsp;</b>are when reflected light rays converge at a point <u><i>infront </i>of the mirror</u>&nbsp;(always <i>inverted</i>)."	"In optics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>real</b></span>&nbsp;<b>images&nbsp;</b>are when reflected light rays converge at a point <u><i>infront </i>of the mirror</u>&nbsp;(always <i>inverted</i>).
