<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>n</b>&nbsp;= refractive index;&nbsp;<b>c</b>&nbsp;= 3 x 10<sup>8</sup>;&nbsp;<b>v</b>&nbsp;= speed of light passing through the medium.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3dc98e3fa2ad6c73c1f89a3fb704febe661dfe5f.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b1cfc6fbc9b9cd601c46901020761ffdfce3d404.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Total&#x20;internal&#x20;reflection&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[phenomenon]</span> of light describes a critical angle (θ<sub>c</sub>)&nbsp;<i>large</i> enough to ensure the refracted ray of light is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>unable</i> to leave the medium</u></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Total internal reflection</b></span> of light describes a critical angle (θ<sub>c</sub>)&nbsp;<i>large</i> enough to ensure the refracted ray of light is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>unable</i> to leave the medium</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Happens when the incident angle is greater than the <b>critical angle (θ<sub>c</sub>)</b>.</div><div>- When light is refracted out of a slow medium (water) into a faster medium (air), the refracted angle is larger than the angle it hit at. If the incident angle is big enough, eventually the refracted angle is so big that it never leaves the medium. This is the <b>critical angle (θ<sub>c</sub>)</b>.</div><div>-&nbsp;<a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WRuatAcd2WY"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WRuatAcd2WY</a><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-4faf969e5c2de113f8edddac1885dfbb5a048b15.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-18081221999daf6b4ce117107334970db27b170c.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Total internal reflection</b></span> of light describes a critical angle (θ<sub>c</sub>)&nbsp;<i>large</i> enough to ensure the refracted ray of light is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;unable&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;leave&#x20;the&#x20;medium&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Total internal reflection</b></span> of light describes a critical angle (θ<sub>c</sub>)&nbsp;<i>large</i> enough to ensure the refracted ray of light is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>unable</i> to leave the medium</u></span>.</div>

"In light refraction, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>critical angle (θ<sub>c</sub>)</b></span> is an&nbsp;<u>incident angle</u>&nbsp;that is&nbsp;<i>large</i>&nbsp;enough to make the refracted ray of light be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">exactly 90° with the medium</span>.

"In light refraction, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>critical angle (θ<sub>c</sub>)</b></span> is an&nbsp;<u>incident angle</u>&nbsp;that is&nbsp;<i>large</i>&nbsp;enough to make the refracted ray of light be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">exactly 90° with the medium</span>.

<div>- n<sub>1</sub> &amp; n<sub>2</sub> refer to <b>indeces of refraction (n)</b>.</div><div>- When light is refracted out of a slow medium (water) into a faster medium (air), the refracted angle is larger than the angle it hit at. <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Converging lenses refract light to a single converging point due to the principles of light refraction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e1a952370f92d23a3f9ae284863d369c8ed65de9.jpg""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Diverging</b></span> <b>lenses</b> are <i>thinner&nbsp;</i>towards their centers.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Diverging lenses refract light outwards due to the principles of light refraction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e1a952370f92d23a3f9ae284863d369c8ed65de9.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation that describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;focal&#x20;length&#x20;&#x28;f&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;real&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;lens"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> (with actual thickness):</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-68361c96dbbefdc91d988abb1b47d6ee0cbe9b39.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation that describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>focal length (f)</b></span> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>real</i> lens</span> (with actual thickness):</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-68361c96dbbefdc91d988abb1b47d6ee0cbe9b39.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Called the <b>lensmaker's equation</b>.<div>- <b>f</b> = focal length; <b>n</b> = refractive index; <b>r<sub>1</sub></b> = radius of the first len's curvature; <b>r<sub>2</sub></b> = radius of the second len's curvature.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-f0f5da161351f4e0dc7ff358869a535a303d32e0.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"In geometric optics, when the object &amp; image are on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>opposite</i></span> side(s) of a <u>lens</u>, the image is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""real"" data-ordinal=""1"">[virtual/real]</span>."	"In geometric optics, when the object &amp; image are on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>opposite</i></span> side(s) of a <u>lens</u>, the image is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">real</span>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Myopia&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[condition]</span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;near&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><u>sightedness</u>, which requires a corrective <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>diverging</i></span> lens in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weaken</i></span> the lens power of the eye."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Myopia</b></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>near</u></span><u>sightedness</u>, which requires a corrective <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>diverging</i></span> lens in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weaken</i></span> the lens power of the eye.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Myopia is a condition where light converges at a point in front of the retina. Glasses with diverging lenses are used to ""weaken"" our eye's lens power in order to allow it to focus on the retina.</div><div>- If the lens has a weak power and has a harder time converging light, its focal length with be long. To represent that its power is small, take its reciprocal.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-153dc861cc4fde84a067591005c8255fbecaeba0.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2ad048560a86a01cb77c9ef93f0f2e02101d7489.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Myopia</b></span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>near</u></span><u>sightedness</u>, which requires a corrective <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;diverging&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[converging/diverging]</span> lens in order to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;weaken&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[strengthen/weaken]</span> the lens power of the eye."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Myopia</b></span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>near</u></span><u>sightedness</u>, which requires a corrective <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>diverging</i></span> lens in order to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weaken</i></span> the lens power of the eye.

<div>- If the lens has a weak power and has a harder time converging light, its focal length with be long. "<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hyperopia</b></span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>far</u></span><u>sightedness</u>, which requires a corrective <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>converging</i></span> lens in order to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>strengthen</i></span> the lens power of the eye.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- If the lens has a weak power and has a harder time converging light, its focal length with be long. To represent that its power is small, take its reciprocal.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-153dc861cc4fde84a067591005c8255fbecaeba0.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2ad048560a86a01cb77c9ef93f0f2e02101d7489.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Hyperopia&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[myopia/hyperopia]</span> is a vision condition where light converges at a point <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>behind</i></span> the retina due to the lens power being too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">weak</span> or the eye being too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">short</span> - requiring <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>converging</u></span> glasses.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hyperopia</b></span> is a vision condition where light converges at a point <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>behind</i></span> the retina due to the lens power being too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">weak</span> or the eye being too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">short</span> - requiring <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>converging</u></span> glasses.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Hyperopia</b> is also called <u>farsightedness</u>.</div><div>- <b>Lens power (P)</b> is the reciprocal of the focal length (f), since it represents the ability of the lens to converge light at a point.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Converging</b>&nbsp;glasses help&nbsp;<u>offset</u>&nbsp;the weakness of the lens - allowing it to focus on the retina.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-153dc861cc4fde84a067591005c8255fbecaeba0.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2ad048560a86a01cb77c9ef93f0f2e02101d7489.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hyperopia</b></span> is a vision condition where light converges at a point <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;behind&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[in front/behind]</span> the retina due to the lens power being too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">weak</span> or the eye being too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">short</span> - requiring <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>converging</u></span> glasses.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hyperopia</b></span> is a vision condition where light converges at a point <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>behind</i></span> the retina due to the lens power being too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">weak</span> or the eye being too <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">short</span> - requiring <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>converging</u></span> glasses.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Hyperopia</b> is also called <u>farsightedness</u>.</div><div>- <b>Lens power (P)</b> is the reciprocal of the focal length (f), since it represents the ability of the lens to converge light at a point.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Converging</b>&nbsp;glasses help&nbsp;<u>offset</u>&nbsp;the weakness of the lens - allowing it to focus on the retina.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-153dc861cc4fde84a067591005c8255fbecaeba0.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2ad048560a86a01cb77c9ef93f0f2e02101d7489.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hyperopia</b></span> is a vision condition where light converges at a point <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>behind</i></span> the retina due to the lens power being too <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""weak"" data-ordinal=""3"">[strength]</span> or the eye being too <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""short"" data-ordinal=""3"">[length]</span> - requiring <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;converging&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[conv./diverg.]</span> glasses.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hyperopia</b></span> is a vision condition where light converges at a point <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>behind</i></span> the retina due to the lens power being too <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">weak</span> or the eye being too <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">short</span> - requiring <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>converging</u></span> glasses.</div>

"<div>This is a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""converging"" data-ordinal=""1"">[converging/diverging]</span> <u>lens</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6c3e01c3ecb3b6615f631b97471f6845621c149f.jpg""><br>"	"<div>This is a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">converging</span> <u>lens</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6c3e01c3ecb3b6615f631b97471f6845621c149f.jpg""><br>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>Equation to find the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;total&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;magnification&#x20;&#x28;m&lt;sub&gt;total&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;multiple&#x20;lens&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> system:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">m<sub>total</sub> = m<sub>1</sub> x m<sub>2</sub> x m<sub>3</sub> x ...</span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation to find the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>total</i>&nbsp;magnification (m<sub>total</sub>)</b></span> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>multiple lens</u></span> system:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">m<sub>total</sub> = m<sub>1</sub> x m<sub>2</sub> x m<sub>3</sub> x ...</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- To find the total&nbsp;<b>focal length</b>,&nbsp;<u>add</u>&nbsp;the reciprocal of everything up.</div><div>- To find the total&nbsp;<b>power</b>,&nbsp;<u>add</u>&nbsp;everything up (no reciprocals).</div><div>- To find the total&nbsp;<b>magnification</b>,&nbsp;<u>multiply</u>&nbsp;each image's magnification (M<sub>tot</sub>&nbsp;= M<sub>1</sub>M<sub>2</sub>M<sub>3</sub>...).</div><div><sub><br></sub></div><div>&nbsp;<img src=""paste-b4b967021063a24874c63b0299ef1a64d9052a66.jpg""></div></div>"
"<div>Equation to find the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;total&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;focal&#x20;length&#x20;&#x28;f&lt;sub&gt;tot&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a <u>multiple lens</u>&nbsp;system:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-112415b05c2936a92979cba21a440dd41a7a6392.jpg""></span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation to find the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>total</i> focal length (f<sub>tot</sub>)</b></span> of a <u>multiple lens</u>&nbsp;system:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-112415b05c2936a92979cba21a440dd41a7a6392.jpg""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- To find the total <b>focal length</b>, <u>add</u> the reciprocal of everything up.</div><div>- To find the total <b>power</b>, <u>add</u> everything up (no reciprocals).</div><div>- To find the total <b>magnification</b>, <u>multiply</u> each image's magnification (M<sub>tot</sub> = M<sub>1</sub>M<sub>2</sub>M<sub>3</sub>...).</div><div><sub><br></sub></div><div><img src=""paste-fca74ff7d43218f6f17298c106509ae3b5b13389.jpg""></div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- To find the total <b>focal length</b>, <u>add</u> the reciprocal of everything up.</div><div>- To find the total <b>power</b>, <u>add</u> everything up (no reciprocals).</div><div>- To find the total <b>magnification</b>, <u>multiply</u> each image's magnification (M<sub>tot</sub> = M<sub>1</sub>M<sub>2</sub>M<sub>3</sub>...).</div><div><sub><br></sub></div><div><img src=""paste-fca74ff7d43218f6f17298c106509ae3b5b13389.jpg""></div></div>"

<div>- To find the total <b>focal length</b>, <u>add</u> the reciprocal of everything up.</div><div>- To find the total <b>power</b>, <u>add</u> everything up (no reciprocals).</div><div>- To find the total <b>magnification</b>, <u>multiply</u> each image's magnification (M<sub>tot</sub> = M<sub>1</sub>M<sub>2</sub>M<sub>3</sub>...).</div><div><sub><br></sub></div><div><img src=""paste-b4398e20a0ac259fe92e1e4e018106b0628ca064.jpg""><sub><br></sub></div><div><sub><br></sub></div><div><img src=""paste-fca74ff7d43218f6f17298c106509ae3b5b13389.jpg""></div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- To find the total <b>focal length</b>, <u>add</u> the reciprocal of everything up.</div><div>- To find the total <b>power</b>, <u>add</u> everything up (no reciprocals).</div><div>- To find the total <b>magnification</b>, <u>multiply</u> each image's magnification (M<sub>tot</sub> = M<sub>1</sub>M<sub>2</sub>M<sub>3</sub>...).</div><div><sub><br></sub></div><div><img src=""paste-b4398e20a0ac259fe92e1e4e018106b0628ca064.jpg""><sub><br></sub></div><div><sub><br></sub></div><div><img src=""paste-fca74ff7d43218f6f17298c106509ae3b5b13389.jpg""></div></div>"
"The units for <b>lens power (P)</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""diopters&#x20;&#x28;D&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The units for <b>lens power (P)</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">diopters (D)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>P</b>&nbsp;= power;&nbsp;<b>f</b>&nbsp;= focal length (meters).<br></div><div>- If the lens has a weak power and has a harder time converging light, its focal length with be long. To represent that its power is small, take its reciprocal.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-153dc861cc4fde84a067591005c8255fbecaeba0.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b4398e20a0ac259fe92e1e4e018106b0628ca064.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In geometric optics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;spherical&#x20;abberrations&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> happen when light <i>fails</i> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>perfectly</i> converge at a point</span> - creating blurred images."	"In geometric optics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>spherical abberrations</b></span> happen when light <i>fails</i> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>perfectly</i> converge at a point</span> - creating blurred images.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This only happens with <u>spheres</u> (lenses &amp; spherical mirrors).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a623574a41eb64f8d473316888a3c5867d73eb4d.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-295c34b3421fbd09ffdfc5dc51173f32fedd1244.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In geometric optics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>spherical abberrations</b></span> happen when light <i>fails</i> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;perfectly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;converge&#x20;at&#x20;a&#x20;point"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - creating blurred images."	"In geometric optics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>spherical abberrations</b></span> happen when light <i>fails</i> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>perfectly</i> converge at a point</span> - creating blurred images.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This only happens with <u>spheres</u> (lenses &amp; spherical mirrors).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a623574a41eb64f8d473316888a3c5867d73eb4d.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-295c34b3421fbd09ffdfc5dc51173f32fedd1244.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In light refraction, waves of light with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;smaller&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[smaller/larger]</span> <u>wavelengths</u> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>larger</i></span> <u>refractive indices (n)</u>."	"In light refraction, waves of light with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i></span> <u>wavelengths</u> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>larger</i></span> <u>refractive indices (n)</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Each different wave of light (corresponding to different colors) have different refractive indices (since they have different wavelengths). Light therefore ""splits"" apart when passed through a prism, since they bend to slightly different degrees.</div><div>- The index of refraction (n) is a function of wavelength, and they're inversely proportional to each other.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2a8745f8f691c0dbe6d845f693dce8761eefa500.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-73cd011d15ecabc8dc6911d660d1b8ae8d17528b.jpg""><br></div>"
"In light refraction, waves of light with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i></span> <u>wavelengths</u> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;larger&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[smaller/larger]</span> <u>refractive indices (n)</u>."	"In light refraction, waves of light with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i></span> <u>wavelengths</u> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>larger</i></span> <u>refractive indices (n)</u>.

<div>- Each different wave of light (corresponding to different colors) have different refractive indices (since they have different wavelengths). Light therefore ""splits"" apart when passed through a prism, since they bend to slightly different degrees.</div><div>- The index of refraction (n) is a function of wavelength, and they're inversely proportional to each other.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2a8745f8f691c0dbe6d845f693dce8761eefa500.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-73cd011d15ecabc8dc6911d660d1b8ae8d17528b.jpg""><br></div>"

Light therefore ""splits"" apart when passed through a prism, since they bend to slightly different degrees.</div><div>- The index of refraction (n) is a function of wavelength, and they're inversely proportional to each other.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2a8745f8f691c0dbe6d845f693dce8761eefa500.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-73cd011d15ecabc8dc6911d660d1b8ae8d17528b.jpg""><br></div>"
"In light refraction, waves of light with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> <u>wavelengths</u> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;smaller&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[smaller/larger]</span> <u>refractive indices (n)</u>."	"In light refraction, waves of light with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> <u>wavelengths</u> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>smaller</i></span> <u>refractive indices (n)</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Each different wave of light (corresponding to different colors) have different refractive indices (since they have different wavelengths). Light therefore ""splits"" apart when passed through a prism, since they bend to slightly different degrees.</div><div>- The index of refraction (n) is a function of wavelength, and they're inversely proportional to each other.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2a8745f8f691c0dbe6d845f693dce8761eefa500.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-73cd011d15ecabc8dc6911d660d1b8ae8d17528b.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>How does <b>light dispersion</b> work in a prism when white light is passed through?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""White&#x20;light&#x20;is&#x20;made&#x20;of&#x20;every&#x20;different&#x20;wavelength&#x2E;&#x20;Each&#x20;light&#x20;wave&#x20;has&#x20;a&#x20;different&#x20;color&#x20;&#x28;since&#x20;they&#x20;have&#x20;&lt;u&gt;different&#x20;wavelengths&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x29;&#x2E;&#x20;Refractive&#x20;index&#x20;is&#x20;a&#x20;function&#x20;of&#x20;wavelength&#x20;&#x28;inversely&#x20;proportional&#x29;&#x2C;&#x20;so&#x20;each&#x20;light&#x20;wave&#x20;experiences&#x20;slightly&#x20;different&#x20;bending&#x20;after&#x20;exiting&#x20;the&#x20;medium&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;appearing&#x20;to&#x20;&quot;split&quot;&#x20;apart&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>How does <b>light dispersion</b> work in a prism when white light is passed through?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">White light is made of every different wavelength. Each light wave has a different color (since they have <u>different wavelengths</u>). Refractive index is a function of wavelength (inversely proportional), so each light wave experiences slightly different bending after exiting the medium - appearing to ""split"" apart.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Chromatic&#x20;abberration&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> happens when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">white light <i>disperses</i> (splits apart)</span> in <u>spherical lenses</u> - resulting in us seeing rainbow ""halos"" around the edges of objects."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Chromatic abberration</b></span> happens when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">white light <i>disperses</i> (splits apart)</span> in <u>spherical lenses</u> - resulting in us seeing rainbow ""halos"" around the edges of objects.

"In the <b>double-slit</b> experiment of light diffraction, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>bright</i></span> spots are where the light's waves interfered <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;constructively&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[const./destruct.]</span>."	"In the <b>double-slit</b> experiment of light diffraction, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>bright</i></span> spots are where the light's waves interfered <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>constructively</u></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Conversely, dark fringes are due to destructive interference (since not much light ended up ""hitting"" the dark paper).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-355bf35d84318964182fd1d2761b158464fbb3c9.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce49aa81f4f9322c1eaf4eabbdb7b9705d95e0a6.jpg""></div>"

"In the <b>double-slit</b> experiment of light diffraction, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>bright</i></span> spots are where the light's waves interfered <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>constructively</u></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Conversely, dark fringes are due to destructive interference (since not much light ended up ""hitting"" the dark paper).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-355bf35d84318964182fd1d2761b158464fbb3c9.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce49aa81f4f9322c1eaf4eabbdb7b9705d95e0a6.jpg""></div>"
"In the <b>double-slit</b> experiment of light diffraction, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>dark</i></span> spots are where the light waves interfered <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;destructively&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[const./destruct.]</span>."	"In the <b>double-slit</b> experiment of light diffraction, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>dark</i></span> spots are where the light waves interfered <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>destructively</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because not much light ended up hitting the dark paper - hence why it appears dark.</div><div>- Conversely, constructive interference results in bright lights.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-355bf35d84318964182fd1d2761b158464fbb3c9.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce49aa81f4f9322c1eaf4eabbdb7b9705d95e0a6.jpg""></div>"
"In the <b>double-slit</b> experiment of light diffraction, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;dark&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[dark/bright]</span> spots are where the light waves interfered <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>destructively</u></span>."	"In the <b>double-slit</b> experiment of light diffraction, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>dark</i></span> spots are where the light waves interfered <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>destructively</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because not much light ended up hitting the dark paper - hence why it appears dark.</div><div>- Conversely, constructive interference results in bright lights.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-355bf35d84318964182fd1d2761b158464fbb3c9.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce49aa81f4f9322c1eaf4eabbdb7b9705d95e0a6.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>d</b> = distance between the two slits; <b>θ</b> = line from the midpoint between the two slits to the dark fringe; <b>λ</b> = incident light's wavelength.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-ce49aa81f4f9322c1eaf4eabbdb7b9705d95e0a6.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Diffraction&#x20;grating&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is a type of light diffraction where <i>many</i> small slits are lined up - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">""color splitting""</span> due to the <u>interference</u> of the diffracted light from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>non-adjacent</i> slits</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Diffraction grating</b></span> is a type of light diffraction where <i>many</i> small slits are lined up - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">""color splitting""</span> due to the <u>interference</u> of the diffracted light from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>non-adjacent</i> slits</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Color splitting only works with <b>polychromatic light</b> (like white light).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-69aef0bc800439bb2c1d7e822f98a6f7ada812bc.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Diffraction grating</b></span> is a type of light diffraction where <i>many</i> small slits are lined up - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">""color splitting""</span> due to the <u>interference</u> of the diffracted light from <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;non&#x2D;adjacent&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;slits"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Diffraction grating</b></span> is a type of light diffraction where <i>many</i> small slits are lined up - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">""color splitting""</span> due to the <u>interference</u> of the diffracted light from <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>non-adjacent</i> slits</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Color splitting only works with <b>polychromatic light</b> (like white light).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-69aef0bc800439bb2c1d7e822f98a6f7ada812bc.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Plane-polarimeters</b></span>&nbsp;<i>polarize</i> light by <i>aligning</i> each light wave's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;electric&#x20;field&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[magnetic/electric]</span> vectors along an axis, while <i>absorbing</i> its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;magnetic&#x20;field&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[magnetic/electric]</span> vectors."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Plane-polarimeters</b></span>&nbsp;<i>polarize</i> light by <i>aligning</i> each light wave's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>electric field</u></span> vectors along an axis, while <i>absorbing</i> its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>magnetic field</u></span> vectors.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Plane polarimeters are said to only polarize the electric field component of electromagnetic waves.</div><div>- Every single light waves' electric field vectors get oriented in the same way with each other (same with magnetic field vectros).</div><div>- Remember, light is on the <u>electromagnetic</u> spectrum, meaning it's composed of both electric fields &amp; magnetic fields.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-dfad7c6b934838b90e9d795e1cbea8216ea90639.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Plane&#x2D;polarimeters&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[equipment]</span>&nbsp;<i>polarize</i> light by <i>aligning</i> each light wave's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>electric field</u></span> vectors along an axis, while <i>absorbing</i> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>magnetic field</u></span> vectors."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Plane-polarimeters</b></span>&nbsp;<i>polarize</i> light by <i>aligning</i> each light wave's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>electric field</u></span> vectors along an axis, while <i>absorbing</i> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>magnetic field</u></span> vectors.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Plane polarimeters are said to only polarize the electric field component of electromagnetic waves.</div><div>- Every single light waves' electric field vectors get oriented in the same way with each other (same with magnetic field vectros).</div><div>- Remember, light is on the <u>electromagnetic</u> spectrum, meaning it's composed of both electric fields &amp; magnetic fields.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-dfad7c6b934838b90e9d795e1cbea8216ea90639.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Circular polarized light</b> results from light waves with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;equal&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[equal/different]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""amplitudes"" data-ordinal=""1"">[wave property]</span> that continually change their directions."	"<b>Circular polarized light</b> results from light waves with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>equal</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">amplitudes</span> that continually change their directions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The <u>average</u> electric field &amp; magnetic field vectors are still <u>perpendicular</u> to each other, like regular electromagnetic waves.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-bf35e569edd49b8083fe8d4c582bf544504154de.jpg""><br></div>"
"In vector addition, use the <u>pythagorean theorem</u> of the x-components &amp; y-components to find the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;resultant&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;vector"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In vector addition, use the <u>pythagorean theorem</u> of the x-components &amp; y-components to find the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>resultant</i> vector</span>.

<div><img src=""paste-9375a4c29ef4bf75738906bf28b04b2369212088.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6bfc3de648c9e25bb8e2fd5d2f8a212280e8027d.jpg""></div>"
"Newton's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;first&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>law</b> says that an object at rest <i>remains</i> at rest, and an object in motion <i>remains</i> in motion, unless acted upon by an <u>unbalanced force</u>."	"Newton's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>first</b></span> <b>law</b> says that an object at rest <i>remains</i> at rest, and an object in motion <i>remains</i> in motion, unless acted upon by an <u>unbalanced force</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-bc048ee068efae8b6944702b1996bec6b50722e5.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-88a866637b2bf3c870443bbd77ad3472cd668bcc.jpg""></div>"
"Newton's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;second&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>law</b> says that force equals mass times acceleration (F = ma)."	"Newton's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>second</b></span> <b>law</b> says that force equals mass times acceleration (F = ma).

<div><img src=""paste-dea4c8242c643c735e9b56cbdbbb789d2bd1a097.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-88a866637b2bf3c870443bbd77ad3472cd668bcc.jpg""></div>"

"Newton's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>third</b></span> <b>law</b> says that every force produces another force <i>equal</i> in magnitude but <i>opposite</i> in sign.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Static&#x20;friction&#x20;&#x28;F&lt;sub&gt;s&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the friction that exists between an object at rest and the surface it's sitting on."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Static friction (F<sub>s</sub>)</b></span> is the friction that exists between an object at rest and the surface it's sitting on.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>F<sub>s</sub> </b>= static friction; <b>µ<sub>s</sub></b> = static friction coefficient (different for every surface); <b>N</b> = normal force (exerted by surface).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8fc577592ce4ade51b928f04ee29860372b84e6c.jpg""></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>F<sub>s</sub> </b>= static friction; <b>µ<sub>s</sub></b> = static friction coefficient (different for every surface); <b>N</b> = normal force (exerted by surface).</div></div>"
"Equation for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;static&#x20;friction&#x20;&#x28;F&lt;sub&gt;s&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-8fc577592ce4ade51b928f04ee29860372b84e6c.jpg""></span><br></div>"	"Equation for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>static friction (F<sub>s</sub>)</b></span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-8fc577592ce4ade51b928f04ee29860372b84e6c.jpg""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>F<sub>s</sub> </b>= static friction; <b>µ<sub>s</sub></b> = static friction coefficient (different for every surface); <b>N</b> = normal force (exerted by surface).</div></div>"
"<div>In the equation for static friction (F<sub>s</sub>), <b>N</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""normal&#x20;force"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-8fc577592ce4ade51b928f04ee29860372b84e6c.jpg""><br></div>"	"<div>In the equation for static friction (F<sub>s</sub>), <b>N</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">normal force</span>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-8fc577592ce4ade51b928f04ee29860372b84e6c.jpg""><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>F<sub>s</sub> </b>= static friction; <b>µ<sub>s</sub></b> = static friction coefficient (different for every surface); <b>N</b> = normal force (exerted by surface).</div></div>"
"<b>Kinetic friction (F<sub>fk</sub>)</b> occurs when an <u>object</u> and its <u>surface</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""slide&#x20;&lt;i&gt;against&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;each&#x20;other"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in motion."	"<b>Kinetic friction (F<sub>fk</sub>)</b> occurs when an <u>object</u> and its <u>surface</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">slide <i>against</i> each other</span> in motion.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Sliding a book over a table → kinetic friction.</div><div>- Tires spinning → static friction (because there's only 1 instantaneous point of contact between the tire and surface - not sliding together).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b788bbf6faa0c6bd6ab845c29aefa43b5e363a30.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d5c38de9c26aaac22855c8de2ee723ec722ad84a.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Kinetic&#x20;friction&#x20;&#x28;F&lt;sub&gt;fk&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[friction]</span>&nbsp;<i>always</i> remains <u>constant</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Kinetic friction (F<sub>fk</sub>)</b></span>&nbsp;<i>always</i> remains <u>constant</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Shown that it's always constant by both the equation's equal sign &amp; graph below.</div><div>- Sliding a book over a table → kinetic friction.</div><div>- Tires spinning → static friction (because there's only 1 instantaneous point of contact between the tire and surface - not sliding together).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b788bbf6faa0c6bd6ab845c29aefa43b5e363a30.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d5c38de9c26aaac22855c8de2ee723ec722ad84a.jpg""></div>"
"<div>The <b>coefficient</b> <b>of</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;static&#x20;friction&#x20;&#x28;&#xB5;&lt;sub&gt;s&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[friction]</span> is <i>always</i> <u>larger</u> than the <b>coefficient of</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;kinetic&#x20;friction&#x20;&#x28;&#xB5;&lt;sub&gt;k&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[friction]</span>.</div>"	"<div>The <b>coefficient</b> <b>of</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>static friction (µ<sub>s</sub>)</b></span> is <i>always</i> <u>larger</u> than the <b>coefficient of</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>kinetic friction (µ<sub>k</sub>)</b></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- It requires more force to set an object in motion (overcoming static friction) than it does to keep it in motion.</div><div>- Sliding a book over a table → kinetic friction.</div><div>- Tires spinning → static friction (because there's only 1 instantaneous point of contact between the tire and surface - not sliding together).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b788bbf6faa0c6bd6ab845c29aefa43b5e363a30.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d5c38de9c26aaac22855c8de2ee723ec722ad84a.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>weight</b></span> of an object:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;274b5572239c46c5135c1af3d6feb4c6d0fc73d9&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>weight</b></span> of an object:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-274b5572239c46c5135c1af3d6feb4c6d0fc73d9.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>m</b> = mass, <b>g</b> = acceleration due to gravity (10 m/s<sup>2</sup>).</div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;weight&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of an object:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-274b5572239c46c5135c1af3d6feb4c6d0fc73d9.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>weight</b></span> of an object:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-274b5572239c46c5135c1af3d6feb4c6d0fc73d9.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>m</b> = mass, <b>g</b> = acceleration due to gravity (10 m/s<sup>2</sup>).</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Mass&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mass/weight]</span> represents the <u>quantity of matter</u> in an object - it is <i>unaffected</i> by gravity."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mass</b></span> represents the <u>quantity of matter</u> in an object - it is <i>unaffected</i> by gravity.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"

<div>Says an object in motion remains in motion, while an object at rest remains at rest, unless acted upon by an external force.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-bc048ee068efae8b6944702b1996bec6b50722e5.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-88a866637b2bf3c870443bbd77ad3472cd668bcc.jpg""></div>"

<div>Every applied force results in an equal but opposite force.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-bc048ee068efae8b6944702b1996bec6b50722e5.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-88a866637b2bf3c870443bbd77ad3472cd668bcc.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-460c6b91de6cf95c1493e19bbc8f8e594e13a0bf.jpg""></div>"
"<div>As an object continues to fall in free fall, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;force&#x20;of&#x20;drag&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[force]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increases</i></span>.<br></div>"	"<div>As an object continues to fall in free fall, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>force of drag</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increases</i></span>.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Once <u>drag</u> equals the <u style="""">gravitational force</u>, <b>terminal velocity</b> is reached.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-460c6b91de6cf95c1493e19bbc8f8e594e13a0bf.jpg""></div>"
"<div>As an object continues to fall in free fall, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>force of drag</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inc/dec/stays constant]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>As an object continues to fall in free fall, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>force of drag</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increases</i></span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Once <u>drag</u> equals the <u style="""">gravitational force</u>, <b>terminal velocity</b> is reached.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-460c6b91de6cf95c1493e19bbc8f8e594e13a0bf.jpg""></div>"
"In <b>projectile motion</b>, the motion's <u>x-component</u> &amp; <u>y-component</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;independent&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[dependent/independent]</span> on/of each other."	"In <b>projectile motion</b>, the motion's <u>x-component</u> &amp; <u>y-component</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>independent</i></span> on/of each other.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>What happens in the x-direction will not have a direct effect on the y-direction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c1bc8631756410aa489802d410ea05710d47f60b.jpg""></div>"
"In <b>projectile motion</b>, the <u>velocity</u> of the object's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">x</span>-component is<i>&nbsp;always</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""constant"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In <b>projectile motion</b>, the <u>velocity</u> of the object's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">x</span>-component is<i>&nbsp;always</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">constant</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the object's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""instantaneous&#x20;velocity"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is always <i>tangent</i> to the circle."	"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the object's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">instantaneous velocity</span> is always <i>tangent</i> to the circle.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-897f5c5262b374b88b6eb22e167b1e7d593995ba.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""centripetal&#x20;force"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> points in the direction towards the <i>center</i> of the circle."	"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">centripetal force</span> points in the direction towards the <i>center</i> of the circle.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Centripetal force <u>is not</u> a force in-and-of itslef. It represents whatever force is responsible for causing it to move in a circular path.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-897f5c5262b374b88b6eb22e167b1e7d593995ba.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""centripetal&#x20;force&#x20;&#x28;F&lt;sub&gt;c&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> represents <i>whatever</i> force is responsible for causing the object to undergo circular motion."	"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">centripetal force (F<sub>c</sub>)</span> represents <i>whatever</i> force is responsible for causing the object to undergo circular motion.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Centripetal force <u>is not</u> a force in-and-of itslef. It represents whatever force is responsible for causing it to move in a circular path.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-897f5c5262b374b88b6eb22e167b1e7d593995ba.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the <b>force</b> <i>tangent</i> to the object's motion is <i>always</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""zero"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the <b>force</b> <i>tangent</i> to the object's motion is <i>always</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">zero</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Tangential force is always zero because the object isn't actually accelerating in the tangential direction... it's only accelerating towards the center of the circle.</div><div>- Centripetal force <u>is not</u> a force in-and-of itslef. It represents whatever force is responsible for causing it to move in a circular path.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-897f5c5262b374b88b6eb22e167b1e7d593995ba.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the <b>velocity</b> of the object <i>doesn't</i> change - it's only considered to accelerate due to <i>continually</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""changing&#x20;direction"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In <u>uniform circular motion</u>, the <b>velocity</b> of the object <i>doesn't</i> change - it's only considered to accelerate due to <i>continually</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">changing direction</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Remember that acceleration is a vector force. Even though velocity (due to the centripetal force) is always constant, direction is always changing (hence accelerating).</div><div>- Tangential force is always zero in uniform circular motion problems.</div><div>- Centripetal force <u>is not</u> a force in-and-of itslef. It represents whatever force is responsible for causing it to move in a circular path.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-897f5c5262b374b88b6eb22e167b1e7d593995ba.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>centripetal force (F<sub>c</sub>)</b></span> in <u>circular motion</u>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>centripetal force (F<sub>c</sub>)</b></span> in <u>circular motion</u>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>m</b> = mass; <b>v</b> = velocity; <b>r</b> = circle's radius.</div><div>- Tangential force is always zero in uniform circular motion problems.</div><div>- Centripetal force <u>is not</u> a force in-and-of itslef. It represents whatever force is responsible for causing it to move in a circular path.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-897f5c5262b374b88b6eb22e167b1e7d593995ba.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;centripetal&#x20;force&#x20;&#x28;F&lt;sub&gt;c&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in <u>circular motion</u>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>centripetal force (F<sub>c</sub>)</b></span> in <u>circular motion</u>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-e914dc8ad67a972d5d0ac193b40acc40c87c7e53.jpg""></span><br>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>m</b> = mass; <b>v</b> = velocity; <b>r</b> = circle's radius.</div><div>- Tangential force is always zero in uniform circular motion problems.</div><div>- Centripetal force <u>is not</u> a force in-and-of itslef. -&nbsp;<b>τ</b> = torque; <b>r</b> = length of the lever arm; <b>F</b> = force; <b>θ</b> = angle between F and the lever arm.<div>- The <b>lever arm</b> is the distance between the applied toque force &amp; the fulcrum.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b039734322a535f0865e406d8465966e2a350e19.jpg""><br></div></div>"

-&nbsp;<b>τ</b> = torque; <b>r</b> = length of the lever arm; <b>F</b> = force; <b>θ</b> = angle between F and the lever arm.<div>- The <b>lever arm</b> is the distance between the applied toque force &amp; the fulcrum.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7767d8dbbdc450205ac74eb9ea09b614adc0fe9e.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b039734322a535f0865e406d8465966e2a350e19.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;second&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[first/second]</span> <b>condition of equilibrium</b> refers to the vector sums of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>torque forces (τ)</u></span> equalling <i>zero</i> on an object." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>τ</b> = torque; <b>r</b> = length of the lever arm; <b>F</b> = force; <b>θ</b> = angle between F and the lever arm.<div>- The <b>lever arm</b> is the distance between the applied toque force &amp; the fulcrum.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7767d8dbbdc450205ac74eb9ea09b614adc0fe9e.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-6d8285c4ddf0c582d4b737cbca03fe34709d3b7e.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>second</b></span> <b>condition of equilibrium</b> refers to the vector sums of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;torque&#x20;forces&#x20;&#x28;τ&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> equalling <i>zero</i> on an object."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>second</b></span> <b>condition of equilibrium</b> refers to the vector sums of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>torque forces (τ)</u></span> equalling <i>zero</i> on an object.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>τ</b> = torque; <b>r</b> = length of the lever arm; <b>F</b> = force; <b>θ</b> = angle between F and the lever arm.<div>- The <b>lever arm</b> is the distance between the applied toque force &amp; the fulcrum.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7767d8dbbdc450205ac74eb9ea09b614adc0fe9e.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-6d8285c4ddf0c582d4b737cbca03fe34709d3b7e.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for <b>kinetic energy</b>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;173ae9a8bd87d4b830b781d449dcbafc59802e1e&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for <b>kinetic energy</b>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-173ae9a8bd87d4b830b781d449dcbafc59802e1e.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>m</b> = mass; <b>v</b> = velocity.</div>"
"The SI unit for <i>all</i> forms of <b>energy</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Joule&#x20;&#x28;J&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The SI unit for <i>all</i> forms of <b>energy</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Joule (J)</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

1 Joule is equivalent to (kg(m<sup>2</sup>))/s<sup>2 </sup>(since it's equivalent to N*m).</div>"
"The unit <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Joule (J)</b></span> is equivalent to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""kg&#x28;m&lt;sup&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x29;&#x2F;s&lt;sup&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[unit]</span>."	"The unit <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Joule (J)</b></span> is equivalent to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">kg(m<sup>2</sup>)/s<sup>2</sup></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- 1 Joule is 1 Newton-meter (Nm).<div>- Notice the similarity between this and what 1 Newton is (kgm/s<sup>2</sup>).</div></div>"
"The unit <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Joule&#x20;&#x28;J&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is equivalent to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">kg(m<sup>2</sup>)/s<sup>2</sup></span>."	"The unit <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Joule (J)</b></span> is equivalent to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">kg(m<sup>2</sup>)/s<sup>2</sup></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- 1 Joule is 1 Newton-meter (Nm).<div>- Notice the similarity between this and what 1 Newton is (kgm/s<sup>2</sup>).</div></div>"
"Equation for the <b><i>gravitational</i> potential energy</b>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""PE&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;mgh"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Equation for the <b><i>gravitational</i> potential energy</b>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">PE = mgh</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>m</b> = mass; <b>g</b> = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>; <b>h</b> = height.</div>"
"Equation for the<b>&nbsp;potential energy</b>&nbsp;in a spring:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""PE&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;1&#x2F;2kx&lt;sup&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Equation for the<b>&nbsp;potential energy</b>&nbsp;in a spring:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">PE = 1/2kx<sup>2</sup></span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- An easier way to calculate this is with <b>W = F<sub>avg</sub>(d<sub>compressed</sub>)</b>.</div>-&nbsp;<b>k </b>= spring constant (stiffness of spring); <b>x</b> = distance it's stretched from its equilibrium point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d8eb8faba35c7e7ca48e34f2f2bf9ff8942797b0.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In the below equation for the elastic potential energy stored in a spring, <b>x</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;distance&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;spring&#x20;is&#x20;pulled&#x20;&lt;i&gt;from&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;its&#x20;equilibrium&#x20;point"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><div>PE = 1/2kx<sup>2</sup></div>"	"<div>In the below equation for the elastic potential energy stored in a spring, <b>x</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>distance</u> the spring is pulled <i>from</i> its equilibrium point</span>.</div><div><br></div><div>PE = 1/2kx<sup>2</sup></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>k </b>= spring constant (stiffness of spring); <b>x</b> = distance it's stretched from its equilibrium point.</div>"
"<div>In the below equation for the <u>elastic potential energy</u> stored in a spring, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>spring constant (k)</b></span>&nbsp;is a measure of the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""spring&#x27;s&#x20;elasticity"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><div>PE = 1/2kx<sup>2</sup></div>"	"<div>In the below equation for the <u>elastic potential energy</u> stored in a spring, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>spring constant (k)</b></span>&nbsp;is a measure of the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">spring's elasticity</span>.</div><div><br></div><div>PE = 1/2kx<sup>2</sup></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>k </b>= spring constant (stiffness of spring); <b>x</b> = distance it's stretched from its equilibrium point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d8eb8faba35c7e7ca48e34f2f2bf9ff8942797b0.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;first&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>law of thermodynamics</b> says <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">energy is <i>always</i> conserved</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>first</b></span> <b>law of thermodynamics</b> says <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">energy is <i>always</i> conserved</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Exemplified by the total mechanical energy of a system: <b>E<sub>tot</sub> = PE + KE</b>.<div>- This is where the equation U = Q - W comes from (conservation of energy in a system).</div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>first</b></span> <b>law of thermodynamics</b> says <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""energy&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;i&gt;always&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;conserved"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>first</b></span> <b>law of thermodynamics</b> says <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">energy is <i>always</i> conserved</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Exemplified by the total mechanical energy of a system: <b>E<sub>tot</sub> = PE + KE</b>.<div>- This is where the equation U = Q - W comes from (conservation of energy in a system).</div></div>"
"<b>Friction</b> is a <u>non-conservative</u> force, meaning a small amount of energy is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lost&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;as&#x20;heat"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Friction</b> is a <u>non-conservative</u> force, meaning a small amount of energy is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lost</i> as heat</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

With non-conservative forces, a small amount of energy is dissipated as <u>heat</u>.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Conservative&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[conservative/non-cons.]</span> <b>forces</b> are forces that <u><i>do not</i> dissipate</u> any energy from the system."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Conservative</b></span> <b>forces</b> are forces that <u><i>do not</i> dissipate</u> any energy from the system.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>A <b>non-conservative force</b> is a force that does dissipate energy from the system, like how friction dissipates energy in the form of <u>heat</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d07a477cf8056148469cadd0baaeba75b7f13525.jpg""></div>"
"The <b>force of drag</b> is a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;non&#x2D;conservative&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[conservative/non-cons.]</span> force."	"The <b>force of drag</b> is a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>non-conservative</u></span> force.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because drag ""inhibits"" the perfect conversion of PE into KE - energy is dissipated.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-460c6b91de6cf95c1493e19bbc8f8e594e13a0bf.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>work</b>&nbsp;done by&nbsp;a <u>non-conservative force</u>&nbsp;in a system</span>:<br></div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""W&lt;sub&gt;non&#x2D;conservative&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;ΔPE&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;ΔKE"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>work</b>&nbsp;done by&nbsp;a <u>non-conservative force</u>&nbsp;in a system</span>:<br></div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">W<sub>non-conservative</sub> = ΔPE + ΔKE</span><br></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

W<sub>non-conservative</sub> represents the ""left-overs,"" since PE and KE will only partially cancel each other out.</div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;work&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;done&#x20;by&amp;nbsp&#x3B;a&#x20;&lt;u&gt;non&#x2D;conservative&#x20;force&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;in&#x20;a&#x20;system"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>:<br></div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">W<sub>non-conservative</sub> = ΔPE + ΔKE</span><br></div></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>work</b>&nbsp;done by&nbsp;a <u>non-conservative force</u>&nbsp;in a system</span>:<br></div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">W<sub>non-conservative</sub> = ΔPE + ΔKE</span><br></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

W<sub>non-conservative</sub> represents the ""left-overs,"" since PE and KE will only partially cancel each other out.</div>"
"<div><b>Non-conservative</b> <b>forces</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;path&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[state/path]</span> functions, meaning its magnitude <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> depend on the path of the system taken.</div>"	"<div><b>Non-conservative</b> <b>forces</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>path</u></span> functions, meaning its magnitude <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does</i></span> depend on the path of the system taken.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"Equation for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;work&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">W = Fdcosθ</span></div>"	"Equation for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>work</b></span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">W = Fdcosθ</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>F </b>= force done on the object; <b>d</b> = distance the object moved; <b>θ</b> = angle between the direction of the force and the direction of the motion.</div>"
"Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>work</b></span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""W&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;Fdcosθ"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div>"	"Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>work</b></span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">W = Fdcosθ</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>F </b>= force done on the object; <b>d</b> = distance the object moved; <b>θ</b> = angle between the direction of the force and the direction of the motion.</div>"
"<div>In the below equation for <u>work</u>, <b>θ</b> represents the <i>angle</i> between the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""applied&#x20;force"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""displacement&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;object"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><div>W = Fdcosθ<br></div>"	"<div>In the below equation for <u>work</u>, <b>θ</b> represents the <i>angle</i> between the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">applied force</span> &amp; the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">displacement of the object</span>.</div><div><br></div><div>W = Fdcosθ<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- If there's no difference between the applied force's angle &amp; the resultant movement (like pushing a box forward), cos(0) = 1.</div>- <b>F </b>= force done on the object; <b>d</b> = distance the object moved; <b>θ</b> = angle between the direction of the force and the direction of the motion.</div>"
"In <u>non-gas systems</u>,&nbsp;<b>work</b> is <i>never</i> done on an object unless the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""object&#x20;&lt;i&gt;moves&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In <u>non-gas systems</u>,&nbsp;<b>work</b> is <i>never</i> done on an object unless the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">object <i>moves</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This <u>doesn't</u> apply to gas systems, though.</div><div>- <b>F </b>= force done on the object; <b>d</b> = distance the object moved; <b>θ</b> = angle between the direction of the force and the direction of the motion.<br></div></div>"
"In <u>gaseous</u> systems, <b>work</b> is only done when the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;volume&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;gas&#x20;changes"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In <u>gaseous</u> systems, <b>work</b> is only done when the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>volume</i> of the gas changes</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>P</b> = applied pressure (that causes change in volume); <b>V</b> = resultant change in volume.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7682b99894e2e77beeaec37532ee3c386cb214dc.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-4acc01aa998ee03d0a3d8870fde2eade3fc9a22e.jpg""><br></div>"
"When the <u>volume</u> of a gas <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;expands&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[expands/compresses]</span>, the <b>work</b> done <i>by</i> the gas was <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive</span>."	"When the <u>volume</u> of a gas <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>expands</i></span>, the <b>work</b> done <i>by</i> the gas was <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive</span>.

"When the <u>volume</u> of a gas <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>expands</i></span>, the <b>work</b> done <i>by</i> the gas was <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""positive"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/-]</span>."
