<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>P</b> = applied pressure (that causes change in volume); <b>V</b> = resultant change in volume.</div><div>- Work can only ever be done when the <u>volume changes</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-4acc01aa998ee03d0a3d8870fde2eade3fc9a22e.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;work&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> done on a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;gaseous&#x20;system&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-7682b99894e2e77beeaec37532ee3c386cb214dc.jpg""></span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>work</b></span> done on a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>gaseous system</u></span>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-7682b99894e2e77beeaec37532ee3c386cb214dc.jpg""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>P</b> = applied pressure (that causes change in volume); <b>V</b> = resultant change in volume.</div><div>- Work can only ever be done when the <u>volume changes</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-4acc01aa998ee03d0a3d8870fde2eade3fc9a22e.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>Equation for&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;power&#x20;&#x28;P&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> as a function of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""work&#x2F;energy"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-34f79514b2ff7ebbe78aa8626d0d84ec2c41513f.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>power (P)</b></span> as a function of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">work/energy</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-34f79514b2ff7ebbe78aa8626d0d84ec2c41513f.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>P</b> = power; <b>W</b> = work (equal to ΔE);<b> ΔE</b> = change in energy; <b>t</b> = time.</div>"
"<div>Equation for&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>power (P)</b></span> as a function of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">work/energy</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;34f79514b2ff7ebbe78aa8626d0d84ec2c41513f&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>power (P)</b></span> as a function of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">work/energy</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-34f79514b2ff7ebbe78aa8626d0d84ec2c41513f.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>P</b> = power; <b>W</b> = work (equal to ΔE);<b> ΔE</b> = change in energy; <b>t</b> = time.</div>"
"The SI unit for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>power</b></span>&nbsp;is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""watt&#x20;&#x28;W&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The SI unit for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>power</b></span>&nbsp;is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">watt (W)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1 watt is equivalent to 1 Joule/second.</div>-&nbsp;<b>P</b> = power; <b>W</b> = work (equal to ΔE);<b> ΔE</b> = change in energy; <b>t</b> = time.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-34f79514b2ff7ebbe78aa8626d0d84ec2c41513f.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The SI unit for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;power&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">watt (W)</span>."	"The SI unit for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>power</b></span>&nbsp;is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">watt (W)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1 watt is equivalent to 1 Joule/second.</div>-&nbsp;<b>P</b> = power; <b>W</b> = work (equal to ΔE);<b> ΔE</b> = change in energy; <b>t</b> = time.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-34f79514b2ff7ebbe78aa8626d0d84ec2c41513f.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The units <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;watts&#x20;&#x28;W&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <i>equivalent</i> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Joules/second</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>The units <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>watts (W)</b></span> are <i>equivalent</i> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Joules/second</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because watts are the SI units for <b>power</b>, and power represents the <u>change in energy over time</u> (or work performed over time).</div>-&nbsp;<b>P</b> = power; <b>W</b> = work (equal to ΔE);<b> ΔE</b> = change in energy; <b>t</b> = time.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-34f79514b2ff7ebbe78aa8626d0d84ec2c41513f.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The units <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>watts (W)</b></span> are <i>equivalent</i> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Joules&#x2F;second"" data-ordinal=""2"">[units]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>The units <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>watts (W)</b></span> are <i>equivalent</i> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Joules/second</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because watts are the SI units for <b>power</b>, and power represents the <u>change in energy over time</u> (or work performed over time).</div>-&nbsp;<b>P</b> = power; <b>W</b> = work (equal to ΔE);<b> ΔE</b> = change in energy; <b>t</b> = time.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-34f79514b2ff7ebbe78aa8626d0d84ec2c41513f.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Mechanically advantageous</b> systems are systems that allow work to be accomplished with a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;smaller&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;force&#x20;input"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, like pulleys or ramps."	"<b>Mechanically advantageous</b> systems are systems that allow work to be accomplished with a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i> force input</span>, like pulleys or ramps.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Results in that force needing to be applied over a much larger distance, though (W = Fdcosθ).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3994461cb2b422101b188f273905d177493a9fdd.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-06f6589688a87d29e539a470a988e6779d51f865.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>Equation to calculate the <b>mechanical advantage</b> due to a <u>simple machine</u>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;3994461cb2b422101b188f273905d177493a9fdd&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation to calculate the <b>mechanical advantage</b> due to a <u>simple machine</u>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-3994461cb2b422101b188f273905d177493a9fdd.jpg""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Mechanical advantage refers to the fact that certain simple machines (like pulleys) require you to only input a small amount of force to do a large amount of work.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-799dcdd0b008cc8802b09fe0b85b96b23488d381.jpg""></div>"
"In order for a <u>large work output</u> to happen due to a <b>mechaically advantageous</b> system (like a pulley), a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""longer&#x20;distance&#x20;is&#x20;required"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to <i>compensate</i> for the smaller force inputted."	"In order for a <u>large work output</u> to happen due to a <b>mechaically advantageous</b> system (like a pulley), a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">longer distance is required</span> to <i>compensate</i> for the smaller force inputted.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- W = Fdcosθ.</div><div>- Mechanical advantage refers to the fact that certain simple machines (like pulleys) require you to only input a small amount of force to do a large amount of work.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3994461cb2b422101b188f273905d177493a9fdd.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-06f6589688a87d29e539a470a988e6779d51f865.jpg""><br></div>"
"In a <u>pulley system</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;effort&#x20;distance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">length of rope</span> that needs to be pulled in order to move the object."	"In a <u>pulley system</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>effort distance</b></span> is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">length of rope</span> that needs to be pulled in order to move the object.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Because two ropes are used in the below image, the <b>effort distance</b> is <u>twice as long</u> to raise the object up when you account for the length of both ropes.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9f524c0120bb9e61c28c87d81069f9f23e63fd77.jpg""><br></div>"
"In a <u>pulley system</u>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>effort distance</b></span> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""length&#x20;of&#x20;rope"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> that needs to be pulled in order to move the object."	"In a <u>pulley system</u>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>effort distance</b></span> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">length of rope</span> that needs to be pulled in order to move the object.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Because two ropes are used in the below image, the <b>effort distance</b> is <u>twice as long</u> to raise the object up when you account for the length of both ropes.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9f524c0120bb9e61c28c87d81069f9f23e63fd77.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;efficiency&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""machine"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Efficiency = W<sub>out</sub>/W<sub>in</sub></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>efficiency</b></span> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">machine</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Efficiency = W<sub>out</sub>/W<sub>in</sub></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- No machine is perfectly ideal (due to energy dissipation, like heat from friction).</div><div>- Because two ropes are used in the below image, the <b>effort distance</b> is <u>twice as long</u> to raise the object up when you account for the length of both ropes.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-88b52d74829195e6e3729efb4d1d3685847982b2.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9f524c0120bb9e61c28c87d81069f9f23e63fd77.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>efficiency</b></span> of a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">machine</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Efficiency&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;W&lt;sub&gt;out&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2F;W&lt;sub&gt;in&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>efficiency</b></span> of a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">machine</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Efficiency = W<sub>out</sub>/W<sub>in</sub></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- No machine is perfectly ideal (due to energy dissipation, like heat from friction).</div><div>- Because two ropes are used in the below image, the <b>effort distance</b> is <u>twice as long</u> to raise the object up when you account for the length of both ropes.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-88b52d74829195e6e3729efb4d1d3685847982b2.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9f524c0120bb9e61c28c87d81069f9f23e63fd77.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>In a <u>pulley system</u>, adding more ropes <i>proportionally</i> <i>decreases</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""amount&#x20;of&#x20;needed&#x20;force"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> but <i>proportionally increases</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""effort&#x20;distance"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> the ropes need to be pulled.</div>"	"<div>In a <u>pulley system</u>, adding more ropes <i>proportionally</i> <i>decreases</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">amount of needed force</span> but <i>proportionally increases</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">effort distance</span> the ropes need to be pulled.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Adding 6 ropes to move an object means it requires 1/6th smaller of a force to move it, but also requires that it'll have to get moved across an effort distance 6 times larger.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-88b52d74829195e6e3729efb4d1d3685847982b2.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f0f45000f0f5dbeb75b4061b747a18ff5f459989.jpg""></div>"
"sin(<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">0</span>°) = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""0"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"sin(<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">0</span>°) = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">0</span>.

"<div>Two <u>like-charges</u> (+) (+) have a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;close&#x20;together&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[close/far apart]</span>.</div>"	"<div>Two <u>like-charges</u> (+) (+) have a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close together</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Both are <u>repelling</u> each other, so the closer together they are, the more electric potential energy.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b887a72d6ab3467f80c043ec5199ea4b628cef36.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fc73dd6548140185c121520d721de07c000ee902.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Two <u>like-charges</u> (+) (+) have a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close together</i></span>.</div>"	"<div>Two <u>like-charges</u> (+) (+) have a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close together</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Both are <u>repelling</u> each other, so the closer together they are, the more electric potential energy.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b887a72d6ab3467f80c043ec5199ea4b628cef36.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fc73dd6548140185c121520d721de07c000ee902.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Two <u>like-charges</u> (+) (+) have a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;far&#x20;apart&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[close/far apart]</span>.</div>"	"<div>Two <u>like-charges</u> (+) (+) have a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>far apart</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Both are <u>repelling</u> each other, so the further apart they are, the less repulsions they each experience.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b887a72d6ab3467f80c043ec5199ea4b628cef36.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fc73dd6548140185c121520d721de07c000ee902.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Two <u>like-charges</u> (+) (+) have a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>far apart</i></span>.</div>"	"<div>Two <u>like-charges</u> (+) (+) have a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>far apart</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Both are <u>repelling</u> each other, so the further apart they are, the less repulsions they each experience.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b887a72d6ab3467f80c043ec5199ea4b628cef36.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fc73dd6548140185c121520d721de07c000ee902.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Two <u>unlike-charges</u> (+) (-) have a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;close&#x20;together&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[close/far apart]</span>.</div>"	"<div>Two <u>unlike-charges</u> (+) (-) have a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close together</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Like-charges are <u>attracted</u> to each other, so they have to do ""less work"" to come together when they're a short distance apart.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b887a72d6ab3467f80c043ec5199ea4b628cef36.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fc73dd6548140185c121520d721de07c000ee902.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Two <u>unlike-charges</u> (+) (-) have a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close together</i></span>.</div>"	"<div>Two <u>unlike-charges</u> (+) (-) have a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close together</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Like-charges are <u>attracted</u> to each other, so they have to do ""less work"" to come together when they're a short distance apart.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b887a72d6ab3467f80c043ec5199ea4b628cef36.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fc73dd6548140185c121520d721de07c000ee902.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Two <u>unlike-charges</u> (+) (-) have a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;far&#x20;apart&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[close/far apart]</span>.</div>"	"<div>Two <u>unlike-charges</u> (+) (-) have a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> <b>electric potential energy</b> when they're <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>far apart</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Like-charges are <u>attracted</u> to each other, so they have to do ""more work"" to come together when they're a far distance apart.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b887a72d6ab3467f80c043ec5199ea4b628cef36.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fc73dd6548140185c121520d721de07c000ee902.jpg""></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"1 <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Newton</span> is <i>equivalent</i> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""kgm&#x2F;s&lt;sup&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>1 Joule is kgm<sup>2</sup>/s<sup>2</sup></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-ee53c3e61843eb07b4165522b8449634573e3f8d.jpg""><br></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"Equation for <b>Hooke's Law</b>&nbsp;in a spring:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""F&lt;sub&gt;elastic&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;&#x2D;kx"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Equation for <b>Hooke's Law</b>&nbsp;in a spring:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">F<sub>elastic</sub> = -kx</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The elastic force (F<sub>elastic</sub>) is the force that points back towards equilibrium (attempting to pull it back).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d8eb8faba35c7e7ca48e34f2f2bf9ff8942797b0.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In Hooke's Law, the <b>elastic force (F<sub>elastic</sub>)</b> points <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;towards&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[away from/towards]</span> the spring's <u>equilibrium point</u>.</div>"	"<div>In Hooke's Law, the <b>elastic force (F<sub>elastic</sub>)</b> points <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>towards</i></span> the spring's <u>equilibrium point</u>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Attempts to restore it back to its equilibrium.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d8eb8faba35c7e7ca48e34f2f2bf9ff8942797b0.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>period (T)</u></span> of a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>simple pendulum</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;11a274f4334edc42e3b6a84941a39f3494f83bf3&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>period (T)</u></span> of a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>simple pendulum</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-11a274f4334edc42e3b6a84941a39f3494f83bf3.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>T</b>&nbsp;= period;&nbsp;<b>L</b> = length of rope; <b>g</b> = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>.</div></div>"

"<div>Does <b>gravity</b> do <u>work</u> <i>only</i> when an object is being lowered?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;it&#x20;does&#x20;work&#x20;in&#x20;&lt;i&gt;both&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;up&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;down&#x20;directions&#x2E;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;Upward&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;i&gt;negative&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;work&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;downward&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;i&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;work&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Does <b>gravity</b> do <u>work</u> <i>only</i> when an object is being lowered?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>No</u>, it does work in <i>both</i> the up &amp; down directions. <u>Upward</u> is <i>negative</i> work &amp; <u>downward</u> is <i>positive</i>&nbsp;work.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>W = Fdcos(θ), where θ is the angle between the force exerted &amp; the displacement of the object.</div><div><br></div><div>- Positive work downwards because θ = 0.</div><div>- Negative work upwards because θ = 90.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-27a9408ddde4d255819d4ff2a5e8ca9cb496fc6d.jpg""></div>"
"The <b>work</b> done by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>force of gravity (F</u><sub><u>g</u></sub><u>)</u></span>:<div><br></div><div>- Object moves upwards: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;negative&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-/0]</span> work is done.</div><div>- Object moves downwards: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-/0]</span> work is done.</div>"	"The <b>work</b> done by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>force of gravity (F</u><sub><u>g</u></sub><u>)</u></span>:<div><br></div><div>- Object moves upwards: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>negative</i></span> work is done.</div><div>- Object moves downwards: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positive</i></span> work is done.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Poiseuille&#x27;s&#x20;law&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[law]</span> describes the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>laminar&nbsp;</i><u>flow rate (Q)</u></span> of an incompressible fluid in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">pipe</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Poiseuille's law</b></span> describes the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>laminar&nbsp;</i><u>flow rate (Q)</u></span> of an incompressible fluid in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">pipe</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Q</b> = flow rate (volume flowing per time); <b>r</b> = tube's radius; <b>L</b> = length of the pipe.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c3f72dd7b09c60138d631dc135d5110b87242dd2.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Poiseuille's law</b></span> describes the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;laminar&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;flow&#x20;rate&#x20;&#x28;Q&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of an incompressible fluid in a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pipe"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Poiseuille's law</b></span> describes the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>laminar&nbsp;</i><u>flow rate (Q)</u></span> of an incompressible fluid in a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">pipe</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Q</b> = flow rate (volume flowing per time); <b>r</b> = tube's radius; <b>L</b> = length of the pipe.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c3f72dd7b09c60138d631dc135d5110b87242dd2.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;pressure&#x20;difference&#x20;&#x28;ΔP&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> on either end of the pipe are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>directly</i></span> proportional.<br></div>"	"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>pressure difference (ΔP)</u></span> on either end of the pipe are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>directly</i></span> proportional.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Increasing the pressure difference results in a greater flow rate (Q).</div><div>- <b>Q</b> = flow rate (volume flowing per time); <b>r</b> = tube's radius; <b>L</b> = length of the pipe.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c3f72dd7b09c60138d631dc135d5110b87242dd2.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>pressure difference (ΔP)</u></span> on either end of the pipe are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;directly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inversely/directly]</span> proportional.<br></div>"	"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>pressure difference (ΔP)</u></span> on either end of the pipe are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>directly</i></span> proportional.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Increasing the pressure difference results in a greater flow rate (Q).</div><div>- <b>Q</b> = flow rate (volume flowing per time); <b>r</b> = tube's radius; <b>L</b> = length of the pipe.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c3f72dd7b09c60138d631dc135d5110b87242dd2.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;resistance&#x20;&#x28;R&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inversely</i></span> proportional.<br></div>"	"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>resistance (R)</u></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inversely</i></span> proportional.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Increasing the pressure difference results in a greater flow rate (Q).</div><div>- <b>Q</b> = flow rate (volume flowing per time); <b>r</b> = tube's radius; <b>L</b> = length of the pipe.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c3f72dd7b09c60138d631dc135d5110b87242dd2.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>resistance (R)</u></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inversely&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inversely/directly]</span> proportional.<br></div>"	"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>resistance (R)</u></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inversely</i></span> proportional.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Increasing the pressure difference results in a greater flow rate (Q).</div><div>- <b>Q</b> = flow rate (volume flowing per time); <b>r</b> = tube's radius; <b>L</b> = length of the pipe.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c3f72dd7b09c60138d631dc135d5110b87242dd2.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>According to&nbsp;<u>Poiseuille's law</u>, the <b>flow rate (Q)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;directly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[inversely/directly]</span> proportional to the radius<sup>4</sup>.<br></div>"	"<div>According to&nbsp;<u>Poiseuille's law</u>, the <b>flow rate (Q)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>directly</i></span> proportional to the radius<sup>4</sup>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Changing the tube's radius has the greatest effect on the flow rate of the fluid through the pipe.</div><div>- <b>Q</b> = flow rate (volume flowing per time); <b>r</b> = tube's radius; <b>L</b> = length of the pipe.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c3f72dd7b09c60138d631dc135d5110b87242dd2.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, changing the pipe's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""radius"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> has the <i>greatest</i> single effect on the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u>.<br></div>"	"<div>According to&nbsp;<b>Poiseuille's law</b>, changing the pipe's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">radius</span> has the <i>greatest</i> single effect on the fluid's <u>flow rate (Q)</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because Q is directly proportional to r<sup>4</sup>.</div><div>- <b>Q</b> = flow rate (volume flowing per time); <b>r</b> = tube's radius; <b>L</b> = length of the pipe.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c3f72dd7b09c60138d631dc135d5110b87242dd2.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Viscosity (η)</b>&nbsp;<i>always</i> points in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;opposite&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;direction&#x20;to&#x20;the&#x20;fluid&#x27;s&#x20;motion"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><b>Viscosity (η)</b>&nbsp;<i>always</i> points in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>opposite</i> direction to the fluid's motion</span>.</div>

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The equivalent resistance (R<sub>T</sub>) is an inverse value.&nbsp;</div><div>- Dividing 1/(large number) gives a low R<sub>T</sub>.</div><div>- Dividing 1/(small number) gives a high R<sub>T</sub>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""37a9e7cd0eec45a3ec6a95b7ff60ba9d.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-44b22787461d92364c5c58b12ca848e68d62a8a0.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The equivalent resistance (R<sub>T</sub>) is an inverse value.&nbsp;</div><div>- Dividing 1/(large number) gives a low R<sub>T</sub>.</div><div>- Dividing 1/(small number) gives a high R<sub>T</sub>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""37a9e7cd0eec45a3ec6a95b7ff60ba9d.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-44b22787461d92364c5c58b12ca848e68d62a8a0.jpg""></div>"
"For resistors in parallel, adding an <i>additional</i> resistor <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> change <u>each individual</u> resistor's <b>current (I)</b>."	"For resistors in parallel, adding an <i>additional</i> resistor <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> change <u>each individual</u> resistor's <b>current (I)</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Even though resistors in parallel have different currents, the <u>addition/removal</u> of a resistor does not affect each individual resistor's current.</div><div><br></div><div>- Resistors in <u>series</u>: each resistor has the same current (different voltage).</div><div>- Resistors in <u>parallel</u>: each resistor has the same voltage (different current).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-44b22787461d92364c5c58b12ca848e68d62a8a0.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Metallic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[metallic/electrolytic]</span> <b>conductivity</b> is the ability of a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>solid metal</u></span> to act as a conductor."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Metallic</b></span> <b>conductivity</b> is the ability of a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>solid metal</u></span> to act as a conductor.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Conductance is the ability for a substance (metal solid or solution) to <u>act as a conductor</u>.</div>"
"In <u>electrolytic conductors</u>, the solution's <b>conductivity</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;directly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[directly/inversely]</span> proportional to its <b>solute concentration</b>."	"In <u>electrolytic conductors</u>, the solution's <b>conductivity</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>directly</i></span> proportional to its <b>solute concentration</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

The more dissolved solutes in solution, the better it can act as a conductor.</div>"

"A battery's <u>potential difference (V)</u> is called the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>electromotive force (ε)</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;before&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[before/after]</span> charge starts to flow."	"A battery's <u>potential difference (V)</u> is called the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>electromotive force (ε)</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>before</i></span> charge starts to flow.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Once charges begin to flow, a <b>current (I)</b> results.</div><div><br></div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Kirchoff&#x27;s&#x20;junction&#x20;rule&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[law]</span> says that the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">total <u>current (I)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>entering</i> a circuit's junction</span>&nbsp;equals the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">total <u>current (I)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>leaving</i> that junction</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Kirchoff's junction rule</b></span> says that the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">total <u>current (I)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>entering</i> a circuit's junction</span>&nbsp;equals the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">total <u>current (I)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>leaving</i> that junction</span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Kirchoff's junction rule</b></span> says that the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""total&#x20;&lt;u&gt;current&#x20;&#x28;I&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>entering</i> a circuit's junction</span>&nbsp;equals the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""total&#x20;&lt;u&gt;current&#x20;&#x28;I&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>leaving</i> that junction</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Kirchoff's junction rule</b></span> says that the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">total <u>current (I)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>entering</i> a circuit's junction</span>&nbsp;equals the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">total <u>current (I)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>leaving</i> that junction</span>.

- Q = flow rate (volume flowing per time)<div>- r = tube's radius</div><div>- L = length of the pipe</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0af1f0d77b0c7947887921c37ce6e9a4efd178b3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Increasing the <u>cross-sectional area</u> of a resistor <i>decreases</i> <b>resistance</b> because it <i>increases</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""number&#x20;of&#x20;conduction&#x20;pathways"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> electrons can take."	"Increasing the <u>cross-sectional area</u> of a resistor <i>decreases</i> <b>resistance</b> because it <i>increases</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">number of conduction pathways</span> electrons can take.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Conduction pathways: different paths the electrons can travel in the current.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-14e9dca69cde9a2ad2d02ef03a93c0bce42c6475.jpg""><br></div><div><img src=""paste-254045e5c4d7e9bf5dc8e33a5a3d3f3e0802bd4b.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In a circuit, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;conduction&#x20;pathways&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are the different <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">pathways&nbsp;<u>electrons can travel</u></span> as they move through the current."	"In a circuit, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>conduction pathways</b></span> are the different <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">pathways&nbsp;<u>electrons can travel</u></span> as they move through the current.
<hr>

"As a <b>current (I)</b> passes through a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>resistor</u></span> in a circuit, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""voltage&#x20;&#x28;V&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[property]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> by some amount after <i>each</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">resistor</span>."	"As a <b>current (I)</b> passes through a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>resistor</u></span> in a circuit, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">voltage (V)</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> by some amount after <i>each</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">resistor</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Drop in voltage across the resistor is equivalent to Ohm's law.</div><div>- Magnitude of the <b>current</b> <u>does not</u> change.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-c566e824f108110ad67be7667d7802231b9e4b5e.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""c5fa09aa5a97459af153e588d7601f91.png""></div>"

"For resistors in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>series</u></span>, the <b>voltage drop</b> across <i>each</i> resistor is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>additive</i></span>.

<div>- Resistors in parallel have equal volatges.</div><div>- Remember, small amounts of voltage is dissipated after each resistor.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3d0d47c9bdfc7a518bc4ab39f3201dd38147fbec.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-acebbc43b82803a93341139fd60f402bf1b2f40f.jpg""><br><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-f57367ffcc0257c061d85f19af6583f4c86c4761.jpg""><br></div></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;total&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;b&gt;voltage&#x20;drop&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> after <i>each</i> resistor in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;series&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[series/parallel]</span>:&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-3d0d47c9bdfc7a518bc4ab39f3201dd38147fbec.jpg""></span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>total</i> <b>voltage drop</b></span> after <i>each</i> resistor in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>series</u></span>:&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-3d0d47c9bdfc7a518bc4ab39f3201dd38147fbec.jpg""></span><br></div>

"For a circuit with resistors in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">parallel</span>, the path with the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>least</i> resistance</u></span> will be <i>preferred</i> by most of the electrons in the <b>current (I)</b>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The greatest proportion of the current will be in part of the path that has the least resistance.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-703203062e7cb799adf52ebb240d69547d839527.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-9e49495c9af85d9dd27719a30946094b8045b38b.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"<div>In the below equation for the capacitance (C) of a parallel-plates capacitor, <b>ε<sub>0</sub></b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;permeativity&#x20;of&#x20;free&#x20;space&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x28;9&#x20;x&#x20;10&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;12&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;F&#x2F;m&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7bf590021238fd8bde556e627ccdc4c192315e6b.jpg""><br>"	"<div>In the below equation for the capacitance (C) of a parallel-plates capacitor, <b>ε<sub>0</sub></b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>permeativity of free space</u> (9 x 10<sup>-12</sup> F/m)</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7bf590021238fd8bde556e627ccdc4c192315e6b.jpg""><br>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>C</b> = capacitance; <b>ε<sub>0</sub></b> = permeativity of free space (8.9 x 10<sup>-12</sup> F/m); <b>A</b> = overlapping area of the plates; <b>d</b> = distance separating the plates.</div>"
"<div>The magnitude of <b>ε<sub>0</sub></b> (permeativity of free space) is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""9&#x20;x&#x20;10&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;12&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;F&#x2F;m"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7bf590021238fd8bde556e627ccdc4c192315e6b.jpg""><br>"	"<div>The magnitude of <b>ε<sub>0</sub></b> (permeativity of free space) is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">9 x 10<sup>-12</sup> F/m</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7bf590021238fd8bde556e627ccdc4c192315e6b.jpg""><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>C</b> = capacitance; <b>ε<sub>0</sub></b> = permeativity of free space (8.9 x 10<sup>-12</sup> F/m); <b>A</b> = overlapping area of the plates; <b>d</b> = distance separating the plates.</div>"
"<div>Equation for the <i>magnitude</i> of an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;electric&#x20;field&#x20;&#x28;E&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> due to a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;potential&#x20;difference&#x20;&#x28;ΔV&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-7d0b75a1005e9c59677e239dbd88a50e98914bcd.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <i>magnitude</i> of an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>electric field (E)</b></span> due to a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>potential difference (ΔV)</u></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-7d0b75a1005e9c59677e239dbd88a50e98914bcd.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>E</b> = electric field; <b>V</b> = voltage; <b>d</b> = distance between the separation of charge.<div>- <u>ΔV = qEΔd</u> → <u>E = ΔV/Δd</u>&nbsp;(E doesn't pay attention to q, since the movement of a charge through the field is only relevant to voltage).<br><div>- Capacitors store energy with the electric field generated between its plates.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-34f8957cc24ce906b582457cf244152d1a8189fa.jpg""><br></div></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>E</b> = electric field; <b>V</b> = voltage; <b>d</b> = distance between the separation of charge.<div>- <u>ΔV = qEΔd</u> → <u>E = ΔV/Δd</u>&nbsp;(E doesn't pay attention to q, since the movement of a charge through the field is only relevant to voltage).<br><div>- Capacitors store energy with the electric field generated between its plates.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-34f8957cc24ce906b582457cf244152d1a8189fa.jpg""><br></div></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>E</b> = electric field; <b>V</b> = voltage; <b>d</b> = distance between the plates.<div>- Points from the positive plate → negative plate.<br><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7d0b75a1005e9c59677e239dbd88a50e98914bcd.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""331f075a39e1afa3b742355ca0ce09cf.png""><br></div><div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-8305a43293ba05ad0a9edca91ce9406df6f869c4.jpg""><br></div></div></div></div>"
"The function of a <b>capacitor</b> is to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;store&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;electric&#x20;charge&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;until it's needed, which is triggered by connecting it to the circuit (closing the switch)."	"The function of a <b>capacitor</b> is to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>store</i> <u>electric charge</u></span>&nbsp;until it's needed, which is triggered by connecting it to the circuit (closing the switch).

"<div>In the equation for the capacitance of some material, <b>Q</b> represents the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""capacitor&#x27;s&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;stored&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;charge&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c7e78d5e954653a6c826d15c0eab07c450344d70.jpg""><br>"	"<div>In the equation for the capacitance of some material, <b>Q</b> represents the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">capacitor's&nbsp;<u><i>stored</i> charge</u></span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c7e78d5e954653a6c826d15c0eab07c450344d70.jpg""><br>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>C</b> = capacitance; <b>Q</b> = stored charge; <b>V</b> = voltage.</div>"
"Adding a <b>dielectric material</b> to a capacitor will always<i> </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreases]</span> its <u>capacitance</u>."	"Adding a <b>dielectric material</b> to a capacitor will always<i> </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increase</i></span> its <u>capacitance</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because dielectric materials are insulators, which can help <u>store</u> the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b53426a16f24caf062ca39522b78c347442df4ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation that describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;new&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;capacitance&#x20;&#x28;C&#x27;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> after adding a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;dielectric&#x20;material&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to a capacitor:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""></span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation that describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>new</i> capacitance (C')</b></span> after adding a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>dielectric material</u></span> to a capacitor:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b53426a16f24caf062ca39522b78c347442df4ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation that describes the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>new</i> capacitance (C')</b></span> after adding a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>dielectric material</u></span> to a capacitor:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation that describes the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>new</i> capacitance (C')</b></span> after adding a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>dielectric material</u></span> to a capacitor:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b53426a16f24caf062ca39522b78c347442df4ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <b>dielectric constant (κ)</b> of a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">vacuum</span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>."	"The <b>dielectric constant (κ)</b> of a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">vacuum</span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b53426a16f24caf062ca39522b78c347442df4ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"The <b>dielectric constant (κ)</b> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">vacuum</span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b53426a16f24caf062ca39522b78c347442df4ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"When a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;dielectric&#x20;material&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""5""><i>isolated</i> from</span>&nbsp;a circuit, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>voltage (V)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> because it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">shields the charges from each other</span>."	"When a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric material</b></span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""5""><i>isolated</i> from</span>&nbsp;a circuit, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>voltage (V)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> because it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">shields the charges from each other</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- According to C = Q/V, this results in an increased capacitance (C).</div><div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""cfd784030804de2f5cb8f05f6a9de34c.png""><br></div></div>"
"When a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric material</b></span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""5""><i>isolated</i> from</span>&nbsp;a circuit, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;voltage&#x20;&#x28;V&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increases/decreases]</span> because it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">shields the charges from each other</span>."	"When a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric material</b></span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""5""><i>isolated</i> from</span>&nbsp;a circuit, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>voltage (V)</u></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> because it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">shields the charges from each other</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- According to C = Q/V, this results in an increased capacitance (C).</div><div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""cfd784030804de2f5cb8f05f6a9de34c.png""><br></div></div>"
"When a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric material</b></span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""5""><i>isolated</i> from</span>&nbsp;a circuit, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>voltage (V)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> because it <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""shields&#x20;the&#x20;charges&#x20;from&#x20;each&#x20;other"" data-ordinal=""4"">[...]</span>."	"When a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric material</b></span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""5""><i>isolated</i> from</span>&nbsp;a circuit, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>voltage (V)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> because it <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">shields the charges from each other</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- According to C = Q/V, this results in an increased capacitance (C).</div><div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""cfd784030804de2f5cb8f05f6a9de34c.png""><br></div></div>"
"When a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;dielectric&#x20;material&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>within</i>&nbsp;a</span>&nbsp;circuit, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>charge (Q)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>increases</i></span> while voltage remains constant (emf source)."	"When a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric material</b></span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>within</i>&nbsp;a</span>&nbsp;circuit, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>charge (Q)</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>increases</i></span> while voltage remains constant (emf source).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is how dielectric materials increase the capacitance of capacitors in circuits.</div><div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b53426a16f24caf062ca39522b78c347442df4ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"When a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric material</b></span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>within</i>&nbsp;a</span>&nbsp;circuit, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;charge&#x20;&#x28;Q&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[increases/decreases]</span> while voltage remains constant (emf source)."	"When a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dielectric material</b></span> is placed between the plates of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>within</i>&nbsp;a</span>&nbsp;circuit, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>charge (Q)</u></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>increases</i></span> while voltage remains constant (emf source).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is how dielectric materials increase the capacitance of capacitors in circuits.</div><div>- Adding dielectric materials increase capacitance, because they act as insulators and can help ""hold"" onto the charge.</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b53426a16f24caf062ca39522b78c347442df4ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"When a <b>dielectric material</b>&nbsp;is placed between the plates of a capacitor&nbsp;within&nbsp;a circuit, the <i>increased</i> <u>charge (Q)</u> is <i>directly</i> proportional to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""dielectric&#x20;constant&#x20;&#x28;κ&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"When a <b>dielectric material</b>&nbsp;is placed between the plates of a capacitor&nbsp;within&nbsp;a circuit, the <i>increased</i> <u>charge (Q)</u> is <i>directly</i> proportional to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">dielectric constant (κ)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- For capacitors within a circuit, the dielectric constant increases the charge (Q) <u>without</u> increasing the voltage (V).</div><div>- <b>C'</b> = new capacitance after dielectric is added;&nbsp;<span style=""font-weight: 700;"">κ</span>&nbsp;= dielectric constant; <b>C</b> = original capacitance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-830f5153c7cf413cad4f8afa64379fe1a4ec7cd8.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b53426a16f24caf062ca39522b78c347442df4ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>For resistors in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;parallel&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[series/parallel]</span>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>removal</i></span> of a resistor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increases</i></span> the total <b>equivalent resistance (R<sub>eq</sub>)</b>.</div>"	"<div>For resistors in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>parallel</u></span>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>removal</i></span> of a resistor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increases</i></span> the total <b>equivalent resistance (R<sub>eq</sub>)</b>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The equivalent resistance (R<sub>T</sub>) is an inverse value.&nbsp;</div><div>- Dividing 1/(large number) gives a low R<sub>T</sub>.</div><div>- Dividing 1/(small number) gives a high R<sub>T</sub>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""37a9e7cd0eec45a3ec6a95b7ff60ba9d.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-44b22787461d92364c5c58b12ca848e68d62a8a0.jpg""></div>"
"<div>For resistors in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;parallel&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[series/parallel]</span>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>addition</i></span> of a resistor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> the total <b>equivalent resistance (R<sub>eq</sub>)</b>.</div>"	"<div>For resistors in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>parallel</u></span>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>addition</i></span> of a resistor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> the total <b>equivalent resistance (R<sub>eq</sub>)</b>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The equivalent resistance (R<sub>T</sub>) is an inverse value.&nbsp;</div><div>- Dividing 1/(large number) gives a low R<sub>T</sub>.</div><div>- Dividing 1/(small number) gives a high R<sub>T</sub>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""37a9e7cd0eec45a3ec6a95b7ff60ba9d.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-44b22787461d92364c5c58b12ca848e68d62a8a0.jpg""></div>"
"For capacitors in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>series</u></span>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;addition&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[addition/removal]</span> of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> the total&nbsp;<b>equivalent capacitance</b><b>&nbsp;(C<sub>T</sub>)</b>."	"For capacitors in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>series</u></span>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>addition</i></span> of a capacitor <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span> the total&nbsp;<b>equivalent capacitance</b><b>&nbsp;(C<sub>T</sub>)</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-81830c34ee566ee4f370c945413cae2cfe3c68dd.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2a7b54301c9bc00e5ef5155556523c03aae1870c.jpg""></div>"
"For capacitors in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>series</u></span>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>addition</i></span> of a capacitor <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increases/decreases]</span> the total&nbsp;<b>equivalent capacitance</b><b>&nbsp;(C<sub>T</sub>)</b>." <div><img src=""paste-81830c34ee566ee4f370c945413cae2cfe3c68dd.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2a7b54301c9bc00e5ef5155556523c03aae1870c.jpg""></div>"
"<div>The <b>equivalent capacitor (C<sub>eq</sub>)</b>&nbsp;of capacitors in series acts as if the <u>distance</u> between its two plates were <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>equal to</i></span> the <u>distance</u> between the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ends&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;series&#x20;capacitors&#x27;&#x20;plates"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>The <b>equivalent capacitor (C<sub>eq</sub>)</b>&nbsp;of capacitors in series acts as if the <u>distance</u> between its two plates were <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>equal to</i></span> the <u>distance</u> between the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ends of the series capacitors' plates</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This would artifically leave a <u>large <b>distance (d)</b></u>, which decreases capacitance... hence why capacitors in series are always smaller.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7bf590021238fd8bde556e627ccdc4c192315e6b.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-81830c34ee566ee4f370c945413cae2cfe3c68dd.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2a7b54301c9bc00e5ef5155556523c03aae1870c.jpg""></div>"
"<div>The <b>equivalent capacitor (C<sub>eq</sub>)</b>&nbsp;of capacitors in series acts as if the <u>distance</u> between its two plates were <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;equal&#x20;to&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> the <u>distance</u> between the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">ends of the series capacitors' plates</span>.</div>"	"<div>The <b>equivalent capacitor (C<sub>eq</sub>)</b>&nbsp;of capacitors in series acts as if the <u>distance</u> between its two plates were <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>equal to</i></span> the <u>distance</u> between the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">ends of the series capacitors' plates</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This would artifically leave a <u>large <b>distance (d)</b></u>, which decreases capacitance... hence why capacitors in series are always smaller.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7bf590021238fd8bde556e627ccdc4c192315e6b.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-81830c34ee566ee4f370c945413cae2cfe3c68dd.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2a7b54301c9bc00e5ef5155556523c03aae1870c.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Ammeters&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are tools used to measure the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>current (I)</u></span> at some point within a circuit."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ammeters</b></span> are tools used to measure the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>current (I)</u></span> at some point within a circuit.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The magnetic field produced by the current exerts a force on the ammeter - changing the positioning of its needle.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b3f7080d825149fb24f8219d9fa424266508ea8e.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-cd7da0df289e2af2bf927a6643f13b2bcbc00032.jpg""></div>"
"<div><b>Ammeters</b> measure the magnitude of <u>currents (I)</u> in circuits by changing the position of its needle depending on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""magnetic&#x20;force&#x20;&#x28;F&lt;sub&gt;B&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> exerted on it - generated by the current itself.</div>"	"<div><b>Ammeters</b> measure the magnitude of <u>currents (I)</u> in circuits by changing the position of its needle depending on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">magnetic force (F<sub>B</sub>)</span> exerted on it - generated by the current itself.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The strength of this magnetic field is used to determing the strength of the current.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-b3f7080d825149fb24f8219d9fa424266508ea8e.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-cd7da0df289e2af2bf927a6643f13b2bcbc00032.jpg""></div>"
"<div><b>Ammeters </b>are inserted in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""series"" data-ordinal=""1"">[series/parallel]</span> with the <u>current (I)</u>.</div>"	"<div><b>Ammeters </b>are inserted in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">series</span> with the <u>current (I)</u>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The magnitude of the current is determined by the strength of the magnetic field the current generates.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-cd7da0df289e2af2bf927a6643f13b2bcbc00032.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Voltmeters</b></span>&nbsp;are tools used to determing the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;voltage&#x20;drop&#x20;&#x28;ΔV&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> across two points on a circuit."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Voltmeters</b></span>&nbsp;are tools used to determing the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>voltage drop (ΔV)</u></span> across two points on a circuit.
