"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ultrasonic</b></span> <b>waves </b>are are sound waves with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>frequencies</u></span> <i>above </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""20&#x20;kHz&#x20;&#x28;20&#x2C;000&#x20;Hz&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[#]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ultrasonic</b></span> <b>waves </b>are are sound waves with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>frequencies</u></span> <i>above </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">20 kHz (20,000 Hz)</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Low frequency → low pitch (bass).</div><div>High frequency → high pitch (whistle).</div><div><br></div><div>- The human hearing threshold is 20 Hz - 20,000 Hz.</div><div><br></div><img src=""184864e066bfe5e01f3acaf1b2b6a17d.gif""></div>"
"<div>When a circuit is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closed</i></span>, a <u>current</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;is&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[is/is not]</span> able to flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6ef01e9263bb860f1a1eb561adc0cd21.png"">"	"<div>When a circuit is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>closed</i></span>, a <u>current</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is</i></span> able to flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6ef01e9263bb860f1a1eb561adc0cd21.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Open switch → current cannot flow.</div><div>- Closed switch → current can flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""0d3784bf6be2b302637cbd2563ee8c88.png""></div>"
"<div>When a circuit is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;open&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[open/closed]</span>, a&nbsp;<u>current</u>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is not</i></span> able to flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6ef01e9263bb860f1a1eb561adc0cd21.png"">"	"<div>When a circuit is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>open</i></span>, a&nbsp;<u>current</u>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is not</i></span> able to flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6ef01e9263bb860f1a1eb561adc0cd21.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Open switch → current cannot flow.</div><div>- Closed switch → current can flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""0d3784bf6be2b302637cbd2563ee8c88.png""></div>"
"<div>When a circuit is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>open</i></span>, a&nbsp;<u>current</u>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;is&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[is/is not]</span> able to flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6ef01e9263bb860f1a1eb561adc0cd21.png"">"	"<div>When a circuit is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>open</i></span>, a&nbsp;<u>current</u>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is not</i></span> able to flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6ef01e9263bb860f1a1eb561adc0cd21.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Open switch → current cannot flow.</div><div>- Closed switch → current can flow.</div><div><br></div><img src=""0d3784bf6be2b302637cbd2563ee8c88.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2B;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[+/-]</span>log(<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x2F;2"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>) is the <i>same</i> as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">-</span>log(<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span>)."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">+</span>log(<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">1/2</span>) is the <i>same</i> as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">-</span>log(<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span>).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

log(1/2) = -log(2) = -0.3.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">+</span>log(<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1/2</span>) is the <i>same</i> as <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2D;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span>log(<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>)."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">+</span>log(<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1/2</span>) is the <i>same</i> as <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">-</span>log(<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span>).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

log(1/2) = -log(2) = -0.3.</div>"
"What's the relationship between +log(___) and -log(___)?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""The&#x20;values&#x20;&lt;i&gt;inside&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;parentheses&#x20;for&#x20;&#x2B;log&#x28;&#x5F;&#x5F;&#x5F;&#x29;&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;&#x2D;log&#x28;&#x5F;&#x5F;&#x5F;&#x29;&#x20;are&#x20;&lt;u&gt;reciprocals&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;each&#x20;other&#x2E;&#x20;&#x2B;log&#x28;1&#x2F;2&#x29;&#x20;is&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;as&#x20;&#x2D;log&#x28;2&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the relationship between +log(___) and -log(___)?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">The values <i>inside</i> the parentheses for +log(___) &amp; -log(___) are <u>reciprocals</u> of each other. +log(1/2) is the <i>same</i> as -log(2).</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"According to Snell's law, when light passes from a medium with a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u> to one with a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">2</sub><u>)</u>, light bends <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>away</i> from</span> the <b>normal line</b>."	"According to Snell's law, when light passes from a medium with a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u> to one with a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">2</sub><u>)</u>, light bends <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>away</i> from</span> the <b>normal line</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-b1cfc6fbc9b9cd601c46901020761ffdfce3d404.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b6d3ad51a2ff0c2e12f309e0135ffd51cc5ff70d.jpg""></div>"
"According to Snell's law, when light passes from a medium with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u> to one with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">2</sub><u>)</u>, light bends <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;away&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;from"" data-ordinal=""2"">[towards/away from]</span> the <b>normal line</b>."	"According to Snell's law, when light passes from a medium with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u> to one with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">2</sub><u>)</u>, light bends <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>away</i> from</span> the <b>normal line</b>.

"According to Snell's law, when light passes from a medium with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u> to one with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <u>refractive index (n</u><sub style="""">2</sub><u>)</u>, light bends <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>towards</i></span> the <b>normal line</b>.

"According to Snell's law, it <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;is&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[is/is not]</span> possible to have a refracted ray of light travel <i>directly</i> along the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">normal line</span>."	"According to Snell's law, it <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>is not</i></span> possible to have a refracted ray of light travel <i>directly</i> along the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">normal line</span>.

"According to Snell's law, it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>is not</i></span> possible to have a refracted ray of light travel <i>directly</i> along the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">normal line</span>.

"Across the <i>same</i> medium, the <b>refractive indices (n)</b> of <i>different</i> light rays <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;can&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> be different - depending on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>wavelength (λ)</u></span> of the specific light ray."	"Across the <i>same</i> medium, the <b>refractive indices (n)</b> of <i>different</i> light rays <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> be different - depending on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>wavelength (λ)</u></span> of the specific light ray.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Refractive index (n) and the light's wavelength (λ) are inversely related.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2a8745f8f691c0dbe6d845f693dce8761eefa500.jpg""><br></div><div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-73cd011d15ecabc8dc6911d660d1b8ae8d17528b.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Across the <i>same</i> medium, the <b>refractive indices (n)</b> of <i>different</i> light rays <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> be different - depending on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;wavelength&#x20;&#x28;λ&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of the specific light ray."	"Across the <i>same</i> medium, the <b>refractive indices (n)</b> of <i>different</i> light rays <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> be different - depending on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>wavelength (λ)</u></span> of the specific light ray.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Refractive index (n) and the light's wavelength (λ) are inversely related.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2a8745f8f691c0dbe6d845f693dce8761eefa500.jpg""><br></div><div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-73cd011d15ecabc8dc6911d660d1b8ae8d17528b.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In light dispersion through a prism, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""violet"" data-ordinal=""1"">[red/violet]</span> light is <u>refracted</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> than <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""red"" data-ordinal=""1"">[red/violet]</span> light due to its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>smaller</i> wavelength (λ)</span>."	"In light dispersion through a prism, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">violet</span> light is <u>refracted</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> than <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">red</span> light due to its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>smaller</i> wavelength (λ)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Violet light is higher in energy than red light. That means violet light has a smaller wavelength, which means it has a larger refractive index (since the refractive index is inversely related to wavelength).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2a8745f8f691c0dbe6d845f693dce8761eefa500.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-73cd011d15ecabc8dc6911d660d1b8ae8d17528b.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"In light dispersion through a prism, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">violet</span> light is <u>refracted</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">red</span> light due to its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>smaller</i> wavelength (λ)</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>f</b> = focal length (distance between focal point &amp; mirror); <b>o</b> = object (thing you're looking at); <b>i</b> = image (what you're actually seeing).<div>-&nbsp;<a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7GV1UZSTNJg"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7GV1UZSTNJg</a><br><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-34b940337312e49d940a8d5feb45f14db06db9d0.jpg""><br></div></div></div>"
"<div>Which equation should you use when you're given either the focal length/image/object distance and are asked to find the <i>positioning</i> of the other components?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;div&gt;Always&#x20;consider&#x20;the&#x20;lens&#x20;equation&#x21;&#x20;This&#x20;equation&#x20;is&#x20;needed&#x20;to&#x20;find&#x20;the&#x20;positioning&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;other&#x20;components&#x2E;&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;&lt;br&gt;&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;7f5bff09da3aa1fb015eecda60a98e7d4248aa5b&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Which equation should you use when you're given either the focal length/image/object distance and are asked to find the <i>positioning</i> of the other components?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><div>Always consider the lens equation! This equation is needed to find the positioning of the other components.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f5bff09da3aa1fb015eecda60a98e7d4248aa5b.jpg""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>f</b>&nbsp;= focal length (distance between focal point &amp; mirror);&nbsp;<b>o</b>&nbsp;= object (thing you're looking at);&nbsp;<b>i</b>&nbsp;= image (what you're actually seeing).<div>-&nbsp;<a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7GV1UZSTNJg"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7GV1UZSTNJg</a><br><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-34b940337312e49d940a8d5feb45f14db06db9d0.jpg""></div></div></div>"
"In geometrical optics, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;focal&#x20;length&#x20;&#x28;f&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a lens describes the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>distance</i> between the <u>lens</u> &amp; the <u>focal point</u></span>."	"In geometrical optics, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>focal length (f)</b></span> of a lens describes the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>distance</i> between the <u>lens</u> &amp; the <u>focal point</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>If someone altered the degree of light bending by a lens, the focal length could be altered. Focal length is not some ""intrinsic property"" of the lens itself - it depends only on where the light rays actually converge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9209b721d0da4e5e0fdc51b7340211291e9f3ea8.jpg""><br></div>"
"In geometrical optics, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>focal length (f)</b></span> of a lens describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;distance&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;between&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;lens&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;focal&#x20;point&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In geometrical optics, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>focal length (f)</b></span> of a lens describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>distance</i> between the <u>lens</u> &amp; the <u>focal point</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>If someone altered the degree of light bending by a lens, the focal length could be altered. Focal length is not some ""intrinsic property"" of the lens itself - it depends only on where the light rays actually converge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9209b721d0da4e5e0fdc51b7340211291e9f3ea8.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>Focal length (f)</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> be altered by placing a lens in a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""new&#x20;medium"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - altering the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;distance&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;from&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;lens&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;focal&#x20;point&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> due to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">relative&nbsp;<i>change</i> in refractive indices</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>Focal length (f)</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> be altered by placing a lens in a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">new medium</span> - altering the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>distance</i> from the <u>lens</u> to the <u>focal point</u></span> due to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">relative&nbsp;<i>change</i> in refractive indices</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: focal lengh is larger when a lens is in water than when it's in air, since the difference between the index of refraction of the lens &amp; water is less than the difference between the lens &amp; air. This smaller difference means there's less of a degree of refraction, meaning the light rays will eventually converge at a further point.</div><div>- <b>Focal length (f)</b>&nbsp;depends&nbsp;only on where the light rays actually converge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Converging-lens20150805-30610-9sjoqh.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>Focal length (f)</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;can&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> be altered by placing a lens in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">new medium</span> - altering the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>distance</i> from the <u>lens</u> to the <u>focal point</u></span> due to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">relative&nbsp;<i>change</i> in refractive indices</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>Focal length (f)</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> be altered by placing a lens in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">new medium</span> - altering the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>distance</i> from the <u>lens</u> to the <u>focal point</u></span> due to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">relative&nbsp;<i>change</i> in refractive indices</span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: focal lengh is larger when a lens is in water than when it's in air, since the difference between the index of refraction of the lens &amp; water is less than the difference between the lens &amp; air. This smaller difference means there's less of a degree of refraction, meaning the light rays will eventually converge at a further point.</div><div>- <b>Focal length (f)</b>&nbsp;depends&nbsp;only on where the light rays actually converge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Converging-lens20150805-30610-9sjoqh.jpg""></div>"
"Patients with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>myopia</b></span> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;shorter&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[shorter/longer]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>focal lengths (f)</u></span> than patients with normal vision."	"Patients with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>myopia</b></span> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>shorter</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>focal lengths (f)</u></span> than patients with normal vision.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Myopia</b>: light converges in front of the retina (shorter focal length (f)) due to either the lens being too powerful or the eye being too long.</div><div>- <b>Focal length (f)</b> is the distance between the lens and the focal point, so it <u>can be modified</u> depending on the medium or the refractive index of the lens itself.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2ad048560a86a01cb77c9ef93f0f2e02101d7489.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: myopia vs. hyperopia.</div><div>- <b>Focal length (f)</b> is the distance between the lens and the focal point, so it <u>can be modified</u> depending on the medium or the refractive index of the lens itself.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2ad048560a86a01cb77c9ef93f0f2e02101d7489.jpg""></div>"
"Objects <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close to</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;round"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">more diverged</span>."	"Objects <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close to</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">more diverged</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Lens power is the ability to refract light rays (with a certain ""strength"") to converge them at a single point. Equation is P = 1/f.</div><div>- Objects close to the eye → greater refraction is required &amp; the&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><img src=""9aea829273036953ee00107fc14156f9.png""></div>"
"Objects <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;close&#x20;to&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[close to/further from]</span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""more&#x20;diverged"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"Objects <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close to</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">more diverged</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Lens power is the ability to refract light rays (with a certain ""strength"") to converge them at a single point. Equation is P = 1/f.</div><div>- Objects close to the eye → greater refraction is required &amp; the&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><img src=""9aea829273036953ee00107fc14156f9.png""></div>"
"Objects <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close to</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lens&#x27;&#x20;power&#x20;&#x28;P&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;focal&#x20;length&#x20;&#x28;f&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">more diverged</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Lens power is the ability to refract light rays (with a certain ""strength"") to converge them at a single point. Equation is P = 1/f.</div><div>- Objects close to the eye → greater refraction is required &amp; the&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><img src=""9aea829273036953ee00107fc14156f9.png""></div>"
"Objects <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close to</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""4"">[increase/decrease]</span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decrease&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""4"">[increase/decrease]</span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">more diverged</span>."	"Objects <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>close to</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">more</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">more diverged</span>.

"Objects <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>further from</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">less</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lens&#x27;&#x20;power&#x20;&#x28;P&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;focal&#x20;length&#x20;&#x28;f&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">mostly parallel</span>."	"Objects <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>further from</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">less</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">mostly parallel</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Lens power</b> is the ability to refract light rays (with a certain ""strength"") to converge them at a single point. Equation is P = 1/f.</div><div>- Objects <u>close to</u> the eye → greater refraction is required (since nearby objects diverge more).</div><div>- Objects <u>further from</u> the eye → less refraction is reqiured (since far away objects have more parallel light rays).</div><div><br></div><img src=""9aea829273036953ee00107fc14156f9.png""></div>"
"Objects <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;further&#x20;from&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[close to/further from]</span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">less</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""mostly&#x20;parallel"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"Objects <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>further from</i></span> the eye force the <b>lens</b> to become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">less</i> round</span> in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>decrease</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>lens' power (P)</u></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>increase</i></span> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>focal length (f)</u></span>, since the incident light rays are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">mostly parallel</span>.

<div><img src=""paste-e762ed6efa7895dc287bcbfc126a0b7533e9e541.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce49aa81f4f9322c1eaf4eabbdb7b9705d95e0a6.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">double</span>-slit experiment, the width of one single slit (a) <i>is not</i> taken into account, because the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""width&#x20;of&#x20;each&#x20;slit&#x20;&#x28;a&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is <i>assumed</i> to be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;smaller&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;than&#x20;the&#x20;light&#x27;s&#x20;wavelength&#x20;&#x28;λ&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">double</span>-slit experiment, the width of one single slit (a) <i>is not</i> taken into account, because the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">width of each slit (a)</span> is <i>assumed</i> to be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i> than the light's wavelength (λ)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The smaller the slit, the more exaggerated the diffraction. This is why making the slit more narrow results in wider patterns.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-355bf35d84318964182fd1d2761b158464fbb3c9.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce49aa81f4f9322c1eaf4eabbdb7b9705d95e0a6.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""double"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>-slit experiment, the width of one single slit (a) <i>is not</i> taken into account, because the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">width of each slit (a)</span> is <i>assumed</i> to be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i> than the light's wavelength (λ)</span>.</div>"	"<div>In the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">double</span>-slit experiment, the width of one single slit (a) <i>is not</i> taken into account, because the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">width of each slit (a)</span> is <i>assumed</i> to be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i> than the light's wavelength (λ)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The smaller the slit, the more exaggerated the diffraction. This is why making the slit more narrow results in wider patterns.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-355bf35d84318964182fd1d2761b158464fbb3c9.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce49aa81f4f9322c1eaf4eabbdb7b9705d95e0a6.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;Both&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;single&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;double"" data-ordinal=""1"">[single/double/both]</span>-slit experiment(s) display <u>interference patterns</u> due to light diffraction."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>Both</i> single &amp; double</span>-slit experiment(s) display <u>interference patterns</u> due to light diffraction.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Single-slit → one light wave interferes with itself.</div><div>- Double-slit → two light waves (nearby) interfere with each other.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""main-qimg-90fc1737cca4df643bca1b25ae41c6e8.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <b>center of mass</b> of an object <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> change when it changes its <u>position</u> - it's ""restricted"" to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;object&#x20;&lt;i&gt;itself&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>center of mass</b> of an object <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> change when it changes its <u>position</u> - it's ""restricted"" to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">the object <i>itself</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Center of mass is ""restricted"" to just the object itself - not its position in space.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-421288eae21454108d292f9e7738f8de5e4568d2.jpg""></div>"
"In order for an object to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">float</span>, its <u>density</u> must be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;smaller&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[smaller/greater]</span> than the density of the <i>surrounding</i> medium."	"In order for an object to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">float</span>, its <u>density</u> must be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>smaller</i></span> than the density of the <i>surrounding</i> medium.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: in extraction, the most dense medium is the one that settles on the bottom, while the least dense medium floats to the top.</div><div>- Ex: hot air balloons, hot air (less dense than cold air) fills the balloon. Since the balloon is at a lower density relative to the surrounding (cold) air, it rises.</div><div><br></div><img src=""83498489e7514a3f14728a928c18fb28.png""><div><br></div><div><img src=""6d39f1cc05221fb84c515d34b8c86ea4.png""><br></div></div>"
"In order for an object to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""float"" data-ordinal=""1"">[sink/float]</span>, its <u>density</u> must be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>smaller</i></span> than the density of the <i>surrounding</i> medium."	"In order for an object to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">float</span>, its <u>density</u> must be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>smaller</i></span> than the density of the <i>surrounding</i> medium.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: in extraction, the most dense medium is the one that settles on the bottom, while the least dense medium floats to the top.</div><div>- Ex: hot air balloons, hot air (less dense than cold air) fills the balloon. Since the balloon is at a lower density relative to the surrounding (cold) air, it rises.</div><div><br></div><img src=""83498489e7514a3f14728a928c18fb28.png""><div><br></div><div><img src=""6d39f1cc05221fb84c515d34b8c86ea4.png""><br></div></div>"
"In order for an object to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">sink</span>, its <u>density</u> must be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;greater&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[smaller/greater]</span> than the density of the <i>surrounding</i> medium."	"In order for an object to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">sink</span>, its <u>density</u> must be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greater</i></span> than the density of the <i>surrounding</i> medium.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: in extraction, the most dense medium is the one that settles on the bottom, while the least dense medium floats to the top.</div><div>- Ex: hot air balloons, hot air (less dense than cold air) fills the balloon. Since the balloon is at a lower density relative to the surrounding (cold) air, it rises.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6d39f1cc05221fb84c515d34b8c86ea4.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""83498489e7514a3f14728a928c18fb28.png""></div>"
"In order for an object to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""sink"" data-ordinal=""1"">[sink/float]</span>, its <u>density</u> must be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greater</i></span> than the density of the <i>surrounding</i> medium."	"In order for an object to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">sink</span>, its <u>density</u> must be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greater</i></span> than the density of the <i>surrounding</i> medium.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: in extraction, the most dense medium is the one that settles on the bottom, while the least dense medium floats to the top.</div><div>- Ex: hot air balloons, hot air (less dense than cold air) fills the balloon. Since the balloon is at a lower density relative to the surrounding (cold) air, it rises.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6d39f1cc05221fb84c515d34b8c86ea4.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""83498489e7514a3f14728a928c18fb28.png""></div>"
"How do hot air balloons work?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Hot&#x20;air&#x20;&#x28;which&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;i&gt;less&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;dense&#x20;than&#x20;cold&#x20;air&#x29;&#x20;fills&#x20;the&#x20;inside&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;balloon&#x2E;&#x20;Since&#x20;the&#x20;inside&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;balloon&#x20;has&#x20;a&#x20;&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;density&#x20;than&#x20;the&#x20;surrounding&#x20;air&#x2C;&#x20;it&#x20;rises&#x2E;&#x20;This&#x20;basic&#x20;principle&#x20;is&#x20;true&#x20;for&#x20;all&#x20;fluids&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"How do hot air balloons work?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Hot air (which is <i>less</i> dense than cold air) fills the inside of the balloon. Since the inside of the balloon has a <i>lower</i> density than the surrounding air, it rises. This basic principle is true for all fluids.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Object's density is smaller than the surrounding medium → floats.</div><div>- Object's density is greater than the surrounding medium → sinks.</div><div><br></div><img src=""83498489e7514a3f14728a928c18fb28.png""></div>"
"<div>As a fluid flows from the valve to point&nbsp;A, the <u>height</u> of the fluid in <b>column</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>I</b></span> is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greatest</i></span>, because it <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""exerts&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;greatest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;pressure"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> due to its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;large&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;cross&#x2D;sectional&#x20;area"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;velocity"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-93343d927c4e47cb55a1ad70d9242aefe114396c.jpg"">"	"<div>As a fluid flows from the valve to point&nbsp;A, the <u>height</u> of the fluid in <b>column</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>I</b></span> is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greatest</i></span>, because it <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">exerts the <i>greatest</i> pressure</span> due to its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>large</i> cross-sectional area</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>low</i> velocity</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-93343d927c4e47cb55a1ad70d9242aefe114396c.jpg"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Bernoulli's equation relates velocity to pressure. When the fliud has a high velocity, it has a low pressure (and vice versa).&nbsp;</div><div>- According to the continuity equation (Q = Av), the velocity in column I is the smallest since it has the largest cross-sectional area. Therefore, column I exerts the largest pressure (largest height).</div><div><br></div><img src=""3153ecf0144e3d1c62b3057678241192.png""></div>"
"<div>As a fluid flows from the valve to point&nbsp;A, the <u>height</u> of the fluid in <b>column</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>I</b></span> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;greatest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[smallest/greatest]</span>, because it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">exerts the <i>greatest</i> pressure</span> due to its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>large</i> cross-sectional area</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>low</i> velocity</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-93343d927c4e47cb55a1ad70d9242aefe114396c.jpg"">"	"<div>As a fluid flows from the valve to point&nbsp;A, the <u>height</u> of the fluid in <b>column</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>I</b></span> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greatest</i></span>, because it <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">exerts the <i>greatest</i> pressure</span> due to its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>large</i> cross-sectional area</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>low</i> velocity</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-93343d927c4e47cb55a1ad70d9242aefe114396c.jpg"">

<div>As the angle increases from 0° to 45°, range increases. As you move past 45° to 90°, the range decreases.</div><div><br></div>- R = vi√(2h/g) → this equation applies only when the angle is 0°.<div>- <b>R</b> = range; <b>v<sub>o</sub></b> = initial velocity (both x- and y-component); <b>θ</b> = angle from which the projectile was launched; <b>g</b> = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>.</div></div>"
"<div>According to the <b>range</b> equation, as the angle&nbsp;<i>increases </i>from<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">45° to 90°</span>, the maximum range <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increases/decreases]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3f7d16a77b604e3a4dc75a406f5e151b8196b05.jpg""><br>"	"<div>According to the <b>range</b> equation, as the angle&nbsp;<i>increases </i>from<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">45° to 90°</span>, the maximum range <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3f7d16a77b604e3a4dc75a406f5e151b8196b05.jpg""><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>As the angle increases from 0° to 45°, range increases. As you move past 45° to 90°, the range decreases.</div><div><br></div>- R = vi√(2h/g) → this equation applies only when the angle is 0°.<div>- <b>R</b> = range; <b>v<sub>o</sub></b> = initial velocity (both x- and y-component); <b>θ</b> = angle from which the projectile was launched; <b>g</b> = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>.</div></div>"
"Two objects fired from 40° and 50° angles, respectively, have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;exact&#x20;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[the same/different]</span> <b>range(s)</b>.<br><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3f7d16a77b604e3a4dc75a406f5e151b8196b05.jpg""><br>"	"Two objects fired from 40° and 50° angles, respectively, have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <i>exact same</i></span> <b>range(s)</b>.<br><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3f7d16a77b604e3a4dc75a406f5e151b8196b05.jpg""><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- These both differ by 5° from 45° - they're complementary angles. They therefore have the same range. See below.</div><div><br></div><div>- As the angle increases from 0° to 45°, range increases. As you move past 45° to 90°, the range decreases.</div><div><br></div>- R = vi√(2h/g) → this equation applies only when the angle is 0°.<div>- <b>R</b> = range; <b>v<sub>o</sub></b> = initial velocity (both x- and y-component); <b>θ</b> = angle from which the projectile was launched; <b>g</b> = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>.</div></div>"
"The <b>range</b> of a projectile <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;is&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[is/is not]</span> affected by the object's <u>mass</u>, since imparting the <i>same</i> kinetic energy to a <i>heavier</i> object results in a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""smaller&#x20;initial&#x20;velocity&#x20;&#x28;v&lt;sub&gt;i&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>range</b> of a projectile <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>is</i></span> affected by the object's <u>mass</u>, since imparting the <i>same</i> kinetic energy to a <i>heavier</i> object results in a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">smaller initial velocity (v<sub>i</sub>)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In order to kick a heavier object the same range as a lighter object, you'd have to impart a higher kinetic energy.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3f7d16a77b604e3a4dc75a406f5e151b8196b05.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Air resistance</b> always points in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;opposite&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[same/opposite]</span> direction as/of the object's motion in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;either&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;x&#x20;or&#x20;y"" data-ordinal=""1"">[x/y/either]</span> direction."	"<b>Air resistance</b> always points in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>opposite</i></span> direction as/of the object's motion in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>either</i> the x or y</span> direction.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>When the object is moving up, air resistance points down. When the object is falling down, air resistance points up. This is why air resistance has no net effect on the time of travel.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c1bc8631756410aa489802d410ea05710d47f60b.jpg""></div>"
"<div><b>Air resistance</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> shorten the amount of <u>time</u> a projectile travels in the air, beause it <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""slows&#x20;&lt;i&gt;both&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;upward&#x20;&lt;i&gt;and&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;downward&#x20;motion"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of the object by equal magnitudes.</div>"	"<div><b>Air resistance</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> shorten the amount of <u>time</u> a projectile travels in the air, beause it <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">slows <i>both</i> the upward <i>and</i> downward motion</span> of the object by equal magnitudes.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>When the object is moving up, air resistance points down (shortening its time moving up). When the object is falling down, air resistance points up (extending its time moving down).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c1bc8631756410aa489802d410ea05710d47f60b.jpg""></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;single&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span><u>-slit light diffraction</u>, light waves that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>differ</i> in wavelength</span> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>1λ/2</b></span> add <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>destructively</i></span> - resulting in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">dark</span> band."	"In <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>single</u></span><u>-slit light diffraction</u>, light waves that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>differ</i> in wavelength</span> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>1λ/2</b></span> add <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>destructively</i></span> - resulting in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">dark</span> band.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This <u>integer difference</u> is indicated by differences in <b>n</b> (nλ/2 → 1λ/2, 2λ/2, 3λ/2, 4λ/2, etc.)</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-63ec36cf4451cb3f497232ddfa49ac5ed4f2e351.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e762ed6efa7895dc287bcbfc126a0b7533e9e541.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;gravitational&#x20;&lt;i&gt;potential&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;energy&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> between two objects:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-9a01952737aa7b830563cedbff2a4267c5e8c5c0.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>gravitational <i>potential</i> <i>energy</i></b></span> between two objects:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-9a01952737aa7b830563cedbff2a4267c5e8c5c0.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- As an object gets further away from the earth, its gravitational potential energy (U) decreases - approaching zero.</div><div>- This is different than the force of gravitation between two objects (F = G(m<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r<sup>2</sup>). This equation <u>does not</u> use r<sup>2</sup>, while the force equation does.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b970cf725a179d75769dfb90b559fa6f4fd7b546.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>gravitational <i>potential</i> <i>energy</i></b></span> between two objects:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;9a01952737aa7b830563cedbff2a4267c5e8c5c0&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>gravitational <i>potential</i> <i>energy</i></b></span> between two objects:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-9a01952737aa7b830563cedbff2a4267c5e8c5c0.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- As an object gets further away from the earth, its gravitational potential energy (U) decreases - approaching zero.</div><div>- This is different than the force of gravitation between two objects (F = G(m<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r<sup>2</sup>). This equation <u>does not</u> use r<sup>2</sup>, while the force equation does.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b970cf725a179d75769dfb90b559fa6f4fd7b546.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Difference between the gravitational <i>force</i> equation &amp; the gravitational <i>potential energy</i> equation:</div><div><br></div><div>Gravitational force: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""F&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;&#x28;Gm&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;m&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&#x2F;r&lt;sup&gt;2&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;→&#x20;&lt;i&gt;does&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;square&#x20;the&#x20;radius&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>Gravitational potential energy: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""PE&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;&#x2D;&#x28;Gm&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;m&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&#x2F;r&#x20;→&#x20;negative&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;square&#x20;the&#x20;radius&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Difference between the gravitational <i>force</i> equation &amp; the gravitational <i>potential energy</i> equation:</div><div><br></div><div>Gravitational force: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">F = (Gm<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r<sup>2 </sup>→ <i>does</i> square the radius.</span></div><div>Gravitational potential energy: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">PE = -(Gm<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r → negative &amp; <i>does not</i> square the radius.</span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- As an object gets further away from the earth, its gravitational potential energy (U) decreases - approaching zero.</div><div>- This is different than the force of gravitation between two objects (F = G(m<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r<sup>2</sup>). "<div>Difference between the gravitational <i>force</i> equation &amp; the gravitational <i>potential energy</i> equation:</div><div><br></div><div>Gravitational potential energy: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""PE&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;&#x2D;&#x28;Gm&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;m&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&#x2F;r&#x20;→&#x20;negative&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;square&#x20;the&#x20;radius&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>Gravitational force: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""F&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;&#x28;Gm&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;m&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&#x2F;r&lt;sup&gt;2&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;→&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;does&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;square&#x20;the&#x20;radius&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Difference between the gravitational <i>force</i> equation &amp; the gravitational <i>potential energy</i> equation:</div><div><br></div><div>Gravitational potential energy: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">PE = -(Gm<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r → negative &amp; <i>does not</i> square the radius.</span></div><div>Gravitational force: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">F = (Gm<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r<sup>2&nbsp;</sup>→&nbsp;<i>does</i>&nbsp;square the radius.</span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- As an object gets further away from the earth, its gravitational potential energy (U) decreases - approaching zero.</div><div>- This is different than the force of gravitation between two objects (F = G(m<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r<sup>2</sup>). "As an object gets <i>further away</i> from the earth's surface, its <b>gravitational <i>potential energy</i></b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&#x28;approaches&#x20;&lt;u&gt;zero&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreases]</span>.<div></div>"	"As an object gets <i>further away</i> from the earth's surface, its <b>gravitational <i>potential energy</i></b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i> (approaches <u>zero</u>)</span>.<div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- As an object gets further away from the earth, its gravitational potential energy (U) decreases - approaching zero.</div><div>- This is different than the force of gravitation between two objects (F = G(m<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r<sup>2</sup>). This equation <u>does not</u> use r<sup>2</sup>, while the force equation does.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-9b7827018e61a6fe799b12c65d00c26937c6c555.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b970cf725a179d75769dfb90b559fa6f4fd7b546.jpg""></div>"
"As an object gets infinitely further away from the earth, its <b>gravitational <i>potential energy</i></b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""maximized"" data-ordinal=""1"">[minimized/maximized]</span> at <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""0"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> J.<div></div>"	"As an object gets infinitely further away from the earth, its <b>gravitational <i>potential energy</i></b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">maximized</span> at <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">0</span> J.<div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Maximized because the equation for gravitational potential energy has a negative sign. As you get infinitely further away, potential energy approaches zero. Zero is the highest possible value (due to the negative sign).</div><div><br></div><div>- As an object gets further away from the earth, its gravitational potential energy (U) decreases - approaching zero.</div><div>- This is different than the force of gravitation between two objects (F = G(m<sub>1</sub>m<sub>2</sub>)/r<sup>2</sup>). This equation <u>does not</u> use r<sup>2</sup>, while the force equation does.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-9b7827018e61a6fe799b12c65d00c26937c6c555.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b970cf725a179d75769dfb90b559fa6f4fd7b546.jpg""></div>"
"The ""apparent weight"" of an object is the <i>same</i> as its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""normal&#x20;force&#x20;&#x28;F&lt;sub&gt;N&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The ""apparent weight"" of an object is the <i>same</i> as its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">normal force (F<sub>N</sub>)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>If you're asked to calculate the apparent weight of something, calculate its normal force.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-5f9ea371f4a50e09d2f363f142329b7de41ebfd9.jpg""></div>"
"Show how you'd calculate the upward <b>normal force</b> exerted on an object if the object was <u>accelerating</u> <i>upwards</i> at 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>...<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;div&gt;F&lt;sub&gt;net&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;ma&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;F&lt;sub&gt;N&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;mg&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;ma&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;F&lt;sub&gt;N&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;ma&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;mg&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;F&lt;sub&gt;N&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;m&#x28;10&#x20;m&#x2F;s&lt;sup&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x29;&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;m&#x28;10&#x20;m&#x2F;s&lt;sup&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;F&lt;sub&gt;N&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;2m&#x28;10m&#x2F;s&lt;sup&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Show how you'd calculate the upward <b>normal force</b> exerted on an object if the object was <u>accelerating</u> <i>upwards</i> at 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>...<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><div>F<sub>net</sub> = ma</div><div>F<sub>N</sub> - mg = ma</div><div>F<sub>N</sub> = ma + mg</div><div>F<sub>N</sub> = m(10 m/s<sup>2</sup>) + m(10 m/s<sup>2</sup>)</div><div>F<sub>N</sub> = 2m(10m/s<sup>2</sup>)</div></span><br></div>

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Simple fluid barometers</b></span> are devices used to measure <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">atmospheric pressure (P<sub>atm</sub>)</span>&nbsp;by measuring the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;height&#x20;of&#x20;water&#x20;in&#x20;a&#x20;tube&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>&nbsp;with one end <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;immersed&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;container"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Simple fluid barometers</b></span> are devices used to measure <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">atmospheric pressure (P<sub>atm</sub>)</span>&nbsp;by measuring the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>height of water in a tube</u></span>&nbsp;with one end <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>immersed</i> in the container</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>P<sub>atm</sub> = ρgh</b> (since P<sub>atm</sub> is roughly equal to hydrostatic pressure in this case).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b4b6a67af12491d50ccb9d8e6945249ecdffc99d.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>Focal length (f)</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> be altered by placing a lens in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">new medium</span> - altering the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>distance</i> from the <u>lens</u> to the <u>focal point</u></span> due to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""relative&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;change&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;in&#x20;refractive&#x20;indices"" data-ordinal=""4"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>Focal length (f)</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> be altered by placing a lens in a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">new medium</span> - altering the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>distance</i> from the <u>lens</u> to the <u>focal point</u></span> due to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">relative&nbsp;<i>change</i> in refractive indices</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: focal lengh is larger when a lens is in water than when it's in air, since the difference between the index of refraction of the lens &amp; water is less than the difference between the lens &amp; air. This smaller difference means there's less of a degree of refraction, meaning the light rays will eventually converge at a further point.</div><div>- <b>Focal length (f)</b>&nbsp;depends&nbsp;only on where the light rays actually converge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Converging-lens20150805-30610-9sjoqh.jpg""></div>"
"<div>When you're given a diagram like this, you <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;cannot&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> estimate <u>focal length</u> based on the graph, because the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""light&#x20;rays&#x20;are&#x20;diverged"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - you <i>must</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""use&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;b&gt;lens&#x20;equation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>!</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-46f2b4bb1734afdd310acc75b2152648b2b3d47f.jpg"">"	"<div>When you're given a diagram like this, you <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> estimate <u>focal length</u> based on the graph, because the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">light rays are diverged</span> - you <i>must</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">use the <b>lens equation</b></span>!</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-46f2b4bb1734afdd310acc75b2152648b2b3d47f.jpg"">

"<div>If the voltometer is 0 V in the center,&nbsp;write the expression for <b>R</b> (on paper).</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;div&gt;1&#x2E;&#x20;Since&#x20;resistors&#x20;in&#x20;series&#x20;have&#x20;the&#x20;same&#x20;currents&#x2C;&#x20;I&lt;sub&gt;R1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;I&lt;sub&gt;R2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;etc&#x2E;&#x2E;&#x2E;&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;2&#x2E;&#x20;Since&#x20;resistors&#x20;in&#x20;parallel&#x20;have&#x20;the&#x20;same&#x20;voltages&#x2C;&#x20;V&lt;sub&gt;R1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;V&lt;sub&gt;R&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;etc&#x2E;&#x2E;&#x2E;&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;3&#x2E;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;Rewrite&#x20;the&#x20;voltages&#x20;in&#x20;terms&#x20;of&#x20;Ohms&#x20;law&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x28;IR&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;I&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;R&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;IR&lt;sub&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;I&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;R&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;4&#x2E;&#x20;Set&#x20;it&#x20;up&#x20;as&#x20;a&#x20;&lt;u&gt;ratio&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;currents&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;and&#x20;solve&#x20;for&#x20;R&#x20;&#x28;R&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;&#x28;R&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;R&lt;sub&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&#x2F;R&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&#x2E;&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-f6d3c631574488c2a83bdef22a6096ad79bf211d.jpg""></div>"	"<div>If the voltometer is 0 V in the center,&nbsp;write the expression for <b>R</b> (on paper).</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><div>1. Since resistors in series have the same currents, I<sub>R1</sub> = I<sub>R2</sub>, etc...</div><div>2. Since resistors in parallel have the same voltages, V<sub>R1</sub> = V<sub>R</sub>, etc...</div><div>3. <u>Rewrite the voltages in terms of Ohms law</u> (IR = I<sub>1</sub>R<sub>1</sub> &amp; IR<sub>3</sub> = I<sub>1</sub>R<sub>2</sub>)</div><div>4. Set it up as a <u>ratio of the currents</u> and solve for R (R = (R<sub>1</sub>R<sub>3</sub>)/R<sub>2</sub>).</div></span><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-f6d3c631574488c2a83bdef22a6096ad79bf211d.jpg""></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-e49b12805a77f527f8def3104bde6c132f60b619.jpg""></div>"
"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;perfectly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;parallel"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b> &amp; <b>focal point</b></span> is/are located."	"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>perfectly</i> parallel</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b> &amp; <b>focal point</b></span> is/are located.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1/f = 1/i + 1/o.</div><div>- Light rays that are&nbsp;<u>perfectly parallel</u>&nbsp;are essentially coming from an object located an infinite distance away. 1/infinity is zero, so the lens equation simplifies to 1/f = 1/i + 0 → 1/f = 1/i → f = i.</div><div>- Light rays that are&nbsp;<u>slightly diverged</u>&nbsp;as they enter the lens do not have objects assumed to be infinitely far away. Therefore, the equation 1/f = 1/i + 1/o canot be further simplified. Focal length and the image distance will be different numbers.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e1a952370f92d23a3f9ae284863d369c8ed65de9.jpg""><div></div></div>"
"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>perfectly</i> parallel</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;light&#x20;rays&#x20;&lt;i&gt;converge&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b> &amp; <b>focal point</b></span> is/are located."	"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>perfectly</i> parallel</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b> &amp; <b>focal point</b></span> is/are located.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1/f = 1/i + 1/o.</div><div>- Light rays that are&nbsp;<u>perfectly parallel</u>&nbsp;are essentially coming from an object located an infinite distance away. 1/infinity is zero, so the lens equation simplifies to 1/f = 1/i + 0 → 1/f = 1/i → f = i.</div><div>- Light rays that are&nbsp;<u>slightly diverged</u>&nbsp;as they enter the lens do not have objects assumed to be infinitely far away. Therefore, the equation 1/f = 1/i + 1/o canot be further simplified. Focal length and the image distance will be different numbers.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e1a952370f92d23a3f9ae284863d369c8ed65de9.jpg""><div></div></div>"
"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>perfectly</i> parallel</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;image&#x20;&#x28;i&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;&lt;b&gt;focal&#x20;point&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> is/are located."	"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>perfectly</i> parallel</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b> &amp; <b>focal point</b></span> is/are located.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1/f = 1/i + 1/o.</div><div>- Light rays that are&nbsp;<u>perfectly parallel</u>&nbsp;are essentially coming from an object located an infinite distance away. 1/infinity is zero, so the lens equation simplifies to 1/f = 1/i + 0 → 1/f = 1/i → f = i.</div><div>- Light rays that are&nbsp;<u>slightly diverged</u>&nbsp;as they enter the lens do not have objects assumed to be infinitely far away. Therefore, the equation 1/f = 1/i + 1/o canot be further simplified. Focal length and the image distance will be different numbers.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e1a952370f92d23a3f9ae284863d369c8ed65de9.jpg""><div></div></div>"
"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>mostly </i>diverged</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;light&#x20;rays&#x20;&lt;i&gt;converge&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b></span> is/are located."	"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>mostly </i>diverged</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b></span> is/are located.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1/f = 1/i + 1/o.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>perfectly parallel</u> are essentially coming from an object located an infinite distance away. 1/infinity is zero, so the lens equation simplifies to 1/f = 1/i + 0 → 1/f = 1/i → f = i.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>slightly diverged</u> as they enter the lens do not have objects assumed to be infinitely far away. Therefore, the equation 1/f = 1/i + 1/o canot be further simplified. Focal length and the image distance will be different numbers.</div></div>"
"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;mostly&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;diverged"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b></span> is/are located." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1/f = 1/i + 1/o.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>perfectly parallel</u> are essentially coming from an object located an infinite distance away. 1/infinity is zero, so the lens equation simplifies to 1/f = 1/i + 0 → 1/f = 1/i → f = i.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>slightly diverged</u> as they enter the lens do not have objects assumed to be infinitely far away. Therefore, the equation 1/f = 1/i + 1/o canot be further simplified. Focal length and the image distance will be different numbers.</div></div>"
"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>mostly </i>diverged</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;image&#x20;&#x28;i&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> is/are located."	"When incoming light rays in a lens are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>mostly </i>diverged</span>, the point where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>light rays <i>converge</i></u></span> is where the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>image (i)</b></span> is/are located.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1/f = 1/i + 1/o.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>perfectly parallel</u> are essentially coming from an object located an infinite distance away. 1/infinity is zero, so the lens equation simplifies to 1/f = 1/i + 0 → 1/f = 1/i → f = i.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>slightly diverged</u> as they enter the lens do not have objects assumed to be infinitely far away. Therefore, the equation 1/f = 1/i + 1/o canot be further simplified. Focal length and the image distance will be different numbers.</div></div>"
"In a lens, explain why the point where the <u><i>light rays converge</i></u> is the location of <i>both</i> the <b>image</b> and <b>focal point</b> when the incoming light rays are <i>perfectly</i> parallel.<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Since&#x20;objects&#x20;emit&#x20;light&#x20;in&#x20;different&#x20;directions&#x20;outwards&#x2C;&#x20;an&#x20;object&#x20;with&#x20;perfectly&#x20;parallel&#x20;light&#x20;rays&#x20;is&#x20;assumed&#x20;to&#x20;be&#x20;of&#x20;an&#x20;infinite&#x20;distance&#x20;away&#x2E;&#x20;In&#x20;the&#x20;lens&#x20;equation&#x20;&#x28;1&#x2F;f&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;1&#x2F;i&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;1&#x2F;o&#x29;&#x2C;&#x20;this&#x20;means&#x20;the&#x20;1&#x2F;o&#x20;term&#x20;is&#x20;zero&#x20;&#x28;1&#x2F;infinity&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;0&#x29;&#x2E;&#x20;Therefore&#x2C;&#x20;1&#x2F;f&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;1&#x2F;i&#x2C;&#x20;which&#x20;means&#x20;f&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;i&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"In a lens, explain why the point where the <u><i>light rays converge</i></u> is the location of <i>both</i> the <b>image</b> and <b>focal point</b> when the incoming light rays are <i>perfectly</i> parallel.<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Since objects emit light in different directions outwards, an object with perfectly parallel light rays is assumed to be of an infinite distance away. In the lens equation (1/f = 1/i + 1/o), this means the 1/o term is zero (1/infinity = 0). Therefore, 1/f = 1/i, which means f = i.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1/f = 1/i + 1/o.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>perfectly parallel</u> are essentially coming from an object located an infinite distance away. 1/infinity is zero, so the lens equation simplifies to 1/f = 1/i + 0 → 1/f = 1/i → f = i.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>slightly diverged</u> as they enter the lens do not have objects assumed to be infinitely far away. Therefore, the equation 1/f = 1/i + 1/o canot be further simplified. Focal length and the image distance will be different numbers.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e1a952370f92d23a3f9ae284863d369c8ed65de9.jpg""></div>"
"In a lens, explain why the point where the <u><i>light rays converge</i></u> is the location of <i>only</i> the <b>image</b>&nbsp;<b>(i) </b>when the incoming light rays are <i>mostly </i>diverged.<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Since&#x20;the&#x20;object&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;i&gt;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;assumed&#x20;to&#x20;be&#x20;infinitely&#x20;far&#x20;away&#x20;&#x28;like&#x20;when&#x20;its&#x20;rays&#x20;are&#x20;perfectly&#x20;parallel&#x29;&#x2C;&#x20;the&#x20;object&#x20;distance&#x20;&lt;i&gt;does&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;matter&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;lens&#x20;equation&#x2E;&#x20;In&#x20;1&#x2F;f&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;1&#x2F;o&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;1&#x2F;i&#x2C;&#x20;f&#x20;and&#x20;i&#x20;are&#x20;different&#x20;numbers&#x2E;&#x20;The&#x20;point&#x20;where&#x20;the&#x20;rays&#x20;converge&#x20;will&#x20;represent&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;b&gt;image&#x20;distance&#x20;&#x28;i&#x29;&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;in&#x20;this&#x20;case&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"In a lens, explain why the point where the <u><i>light rays converge</i></u> is the location of <i>only</i> the <b>image</b>&nbsp;<b>(i) </b>when the incoming light rays are <i>mostly </i>diverged.<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Since the object is <i>not</i> assumed to be infinitely far away (like when its rays are perfectly parallel), the object distance <i>does</i> matter in the lens equation. In 1/f = 1/o + 1/i, f and i are different numbers. The point where the rays converge will represent the <b>image distance (i) </b>in this case.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 1/f = 1/i + 1/o.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>perfectly parallel</u> are essentially coming from an object located an infinite distance away. 1/infinity is zero, so the lens equation simplifies to 1/f = 1/i + 0 → 1/f = 1/i → f = i.</div><div>- Light rays that are <u>slightly diverged</u> as they enter the lens do not have objects assumed to be infinitely far away. Therefore, the equation 1/f = 1/i + 1/o canot be further simplified. Focal length and the image distance will be different numbers.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e1a952370f92d23a3f9ae284863d369c8ed65de9.jpg""></div>"
"Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">slope of a line</span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""y&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;mx&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;b"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">slope of a line</span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">y = mx + b</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>y</b> = y value; <b>m</b> = slope; <b>x</b> = x value; <b>b</b> = y-intercept.<div>- Be able to recognize that some equations are given in the y=mx + b form, such as the one below.<br><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-fd3ecea81aaeacc76e30d0e00e11522e5ea06814.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-1dd2e3b651ca69adae764215bc73efb091093b22.jpg""><br></div></div></div>"

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>
